'js' : 'cjs'}`,\n    },\n    rollupOptions: {\n      external: ['vue', 'motion'],\n      output: { globals: { vue: 'Vue', motion: 'Motion' } },\n    },\n    sourcemap: true,\n    cssCodeSplit: false,\n  },\n})\n```\n\n### Step 1.4: `goey-toast-vue/vitest.config.ts`\n\n```typescript\nimport { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config'\nimport vue from '@vitejs/plugin-vue'\n\nexport default defineConfig({\n  plugins: [vue()],\n  test: {\n    globals: true,\n    environment: 'jsdom',\n    setupFiles: ['src/__tests__/setup.ts'],\n  },\n})\n```\n\n### Step 1.5: `goey-toast-vue/src/__tests__/setup.ts`\n\nEmpty file initially. Will add `@testing-library/jest-dom` or custom matchers if needed.\n\n```typescript\n// Test setup — add global mocks/matchers here\n```\n\n### Step 1.6: Create placeholder `goey-toast-vue/src/index.ts`\n\n```typescript\n// Public API — will be populated in Phase 9\nexport {}\n```\n\n### Step 1.7: `goey-toast-vue/env.d.ts`\n\nVue SFC type shim:\n\n```typescript\n/// <reference types=\"vite/client\" />\ndeclare module '*.vue' {\n  import type { DefineComponent } from 'vue'\n  const component: DefineComponent<{}, {}, any>\n  export default component\n}\n```\n\n**Verification**: `npm install && npm run build` exits 0 (empty lib output).\n\n---\n\n## Phase 2: Pure Functions & Types\n\n**Goal**: Port all framework-agnostic code. These files have zero Vue/React imports.\n\n### Step 2.1: `src/types.ts`\n\nCopy from React `types.ts`. Changes:\n- Remove `import type { ReactNode } from 'react'`\n- Remove `import type { ExternalToast, ToasterProps } from 'sonner'`\n- Replace all `ReactNode` with `VNode | string` (import from `vue`)\n- Replace `ToasterProps['position']` with inline union type `'top-left' | 'top-center' | 'top-right' | 'bottom-left' | 'bottom-center' | 'bottom-right'`\n- Remove `GooeyToasterProps.toastOptions` (Sonner-specific `Partial<ExternalToast>`)\n- Remove `GooeyToasterProps.richColors` (Sonner-specific)\n- Remove `GooeyToasterProps.expand` (Sonner-specific — our ToastContainer handles this)\n- Keep `GooeyToasterProps.visibleToasts` (we implement this ourselves)\n\nFull type list to port:\n- `GooeyToastType` — identical\n- `GooeyToastTimings` — identical\n- `GooeyToastClassNames` — identical\n- `GooeyToastAction` — identical\n- `GooeyToastData` — `ReactNode` → `VNode | string`\n- `GooeyToastOptions` — `ReactNode` → `VNode | string`\n- `GooeyPromiseData<T>` — `ReactNode` → `VNode | string`\n- `GooeyToastPhase` — identical\n- `GooeyToastUpdateOptions` — `ReactNode` → `VNode | string`\n- `DismissFilter` — identical\n- `GooeyToasterProps` — remove Sonner fields, inline position type\n- `ToastPosition` — new exported type alias for the 6-position union\n\n### Step 2.2: `src/presets.ts`\n\n**Direct copy** from React `presets.ts`. Zero changes needed.\n\n```typescript\nexport interface AnimationPreset {\n  bounce: number\n  spring: boolean\n}\n\nexport const animationPresets = {\n  smooth: { bounce: 0.1, spring: true },\n  bouncy: { bounce: 0.6, spring: true },\n  subtle: { bounce: 0.05, spring: true },\n  snappy: { bounce: 0.4, spring: true },\n} as const satisfies Record<string, AnimationPreset>\n\nexport type AnimationPresetName = keyof typeof animationPresets\n```\n\n### Step 2.3: `src/morph.ts`\n\nExtract from React `GooeyToast.tsx` lines 221-360. Three exports:\n- `memoizePath(fn)` — generic last-result cache\n- `morphPath` — memoized `morphPathRaw`\n- `morphPathCenter` — memoized `morphPathCenterRaw`\n- `PH` constant (= 34)\n\nAlso export the raw functions for testing:\n- `morphPathRaw(pw, bw, th, t)`\n- `morphPathCenterRaw(pw, bw, th, t)`\n\n**Zero changes** to the math. Direct copy.\n\n### Step 2.4: `src/spring.ts`\n\nExtract from React `GooeyToast.tsx` lines 77-86. Exports:\n- `squishSpring(durationSec, defaultDur, bounce?)` — returns `{ type: 'spring', stiffness, damping, mass }`\n- `DEFAULT_EXPAND_DUR` (= 0.6)\n- `DEFAULT_COLLAPSE_DUR` (= 0.9)\n- `SMOOTH_EASE` (= [0.4, 0, 0.2, 1])\n\n**Zero changes**. Direct copy.\n\n### Step 2.5: `src/components/gooey-styles.ts`\n\n**Direct copy** from React `gooey-styles.ts`. Zero changes.\n\n**Verification**: `npm run typecheck` passes for Phase 2 files.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 3: State Management\n\n**Goal**: Replace React's module-level variables + pub/sub with Vue reactive primitives.\n\n### Step 3.1: `src/context.ts`\n\nReplace the React version's getter/setter + pub/sub pattern with `reactive`/`ref`:\n\n```typescript\nimport { reactive, ref } from 'vue'\nimport type { ToastPosition } from './types'\n\n// Global config — reactive singleton\nexport const _config = reactive({\n  position: 'bottom-right' as ToastPosition,\n  dir: 'ltr' as 'ltr' | 'rtl',\n  spring: true,\n  bounce: undefined as number | undefined,\n  theme: 'light' as 'light' | 'dark',\n  visibleToasts: 3,\n  swipeToDismiss: true,\n  closeOnEscape: true,\n  maxQueue: Infinity,\n  queueOverflow: 'drop-oldest' as 'drop-oldest' | 'drop-newest',\n  showProgress: false,\n  closeButton: false as boolean | 'top-left' | 'top-right',\n})\n\n// Container hover state — watched by GooeyToast instances\nexport const containerHovered = ref(false)\n\n// ARIA announcements — watched by AriaLiveAnnouncer\nexport type AriaLivePoliteness = 'polite' | 'assertive'\nexport interface Announcement {\n  message: string\n  politeness: AriaLivePoliteness\n}\nexport const _announcement = ref<Announcement | null>(null)\n\nexport function announce(message: string, politeness: AriaLivePoliteness = 'polite') {\n  // Set to null first to trigger watchers even for identical messages\n  _announcement.value = null\n  // nextTick or queueMicrotask to ensure the null is observed before the new value\n  queueMicrotask(() => {\n    _announcement.value = { message, politeness }\n  })\n}\n```\n\nKey difference from React version:\n- No getter/setter functions needed — direct property access on `_config`\n- No `subscribeContainerHovered()` — components `watch(containerHovered, ...)`\n- No `subscribeAnnouncements()` — AriaLiveAnnouncer `watch(_announcement, ...)`\n- `announce()` uses null→value pattern to re-trigger watchers for duplicate messages\n\n### Step 3.2: `src/gooey-toast.ts`\n\nThe most complex state file. Port from React `gooey-toast.tsx` (502 lines).\n\nKey architectural changes:\n1. **No Sonner dependency** — `toast.custom()` / `toast.dismiss()` replaced by reactive array\n2. **No React components** — `GooeyToastWrapper` / `PromiseToastWrapper` are not needed; ToastContainer renders directly from the reactive toast list\n3. **Toast lifecycle managed by reactive data**, not Sonner unmount\n\nData structures:\n\n```typescript\nimport { ref, type Ref } from 'vue'\n\nexport interface InternalToast {\n  id: string | number\n  title: string\n  type: GooeyToastType\n  phase: GooeyToastPhase\n  description?: VNode | string\n  action?: GooeyToastAction\n  icon?: VNode | string\n  duration?: number\n  classNames?: GooeyToastClassNames\n  fillColor?: string\n  borderColor?: string\n  borderWidth?: number\n  timing?: GooeyToastTimings\n  preset?: AnimationPresetName\n  spring?: boolean\n  bounce?: number\n  showProgress?: boolean\n  showTimestamp?: boolean\n  // Promise-specific\n  _promise?: Promise<unknown>\n  _promiseData?: GooeyPromiseData<unknown>\n  // Callbacks\n  _onDismiss?: (id: string | number) => void\n  _onAutoClose?: (id: string | number) => void\n}\n\n// Active toasts visible on screen\nexport const _toasts: Ref<InternalToast[]> = ref([])\n\n// Queue for overflow\nconst _queue: Array<{ id: string | number; type: GooeyToastType; toast: InternalToast }> = []\n\n// Track manual vs auto dismiss\nconst _autoCloseFlags = new Set<string | number>()\nconst _manualDismissFlags = new Set<string | number>()\n```\n\nFunctions to port (with changes noted):\n\n- `createGooeyToast(title, type, options)` — Instead of `toast.custom()`, push to `_toasts.value`. Queue logic same as React.\n- `dismissGooeyToast(idOrFilter?)` — Instead of `toast.dismiss()`, splice from `_toasts.value`. Type-filter and dismiss-all logic same as React.\n- `updateGooeyToast(id, options)` — Find toast in `_toasts.value` by id, mutate fields directly (Vue reactivity picks up changes). No listener registry needed.\n- `_processQueue()` — Same logic, but `create` callback pushes to `_toasts.value`.\n- `_enqueue(entry)` — Same logic.\n- `_onToastDismissed(id)` — Remove from `_toasts.value`, invoke callbacks, process queue.\n- `_markAutoClose(id)` — Same.\n- `_resetQueue()` — Same (clear `_toasts.value`, `_queue`, flags).\n- `_getMostRecentActiveId()` — Return `_toasts.value[_toasts.value.length - 1]?.id`.\n\nPromise toast handling:\n- Instead of `PromiseToastWrapper` component, the `gooeyToast.promise()` function creates an `InternalToast` with `phase: 'loading'` and `_promise` / `_promiseData` fields.\n- A `promise.then().catch()` chain updates the toast in `_toasts.value` when it resolves/rejects — mutating `title`, `phase`, `description`, `action` directly.\n\nARIA announcements:\n- Call `announce()` from `context.ts` in `createGooeyToast()` and `updateGooeyToast()`.\n- `buildAnnouncementMessage()` and `getAnnouncePoliteness()` — direct copy.\n\nPublic API shape — identical to React:\n\n```typescript\nexport const gooeyToast = Object.assign(\n  (title: string, options?: GooeyToastOptions) => createGooeyToast(title, 'default', options),\n  {\n    success: (title, options?) => createGooeyToast(title, 'success', options),\n    error: (title, options?) => createGooeyToast(title, 'error', options),\n    warning: (title, options?) => createGooeyToast(title, 'warning', options),\n    info: (title, options?) => createGooeyToast(title, 'info', options),\n    promise: <T,>(promise: Promise<T>, data: GooeyPromiseData<T>) => { ... },\n    dismiss: dismissGooeyToast,\n    update: updateGooeyToast,\n  }\n)\n```\n\n**Verification**: Unit tests for queue logic, dismiss filters, promise state transitions (Phase 10).\n\n---\n\n## Phase 4: Composables\n\n### Step 4.1: `src/composables/usePrefersReducedMotion.ts`\n\nPort from React `usePrefersReducedMotion.ts`:\n\n```typescript\nimport { ref, onMounted, onUnmounted, readonly, type DeepReadonly, type Ref } from 'vue'\n\nconst QUERY = '(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)'\n\nexport function usePrefersReducedMotion(): Readonly<Ref<boolean>> {\n  const prefersReducedMotion = ref(\n    typeof window !== 'undefined' && typeof window.matchMedia === 'function'\n      ? window.matchMedia(QUERY).matches\n      : false\n  )\n\n  let mql: MediaQueryList | null = null\n  const handler = (e: MediaQueryListEvent) => {\n    prefersReducedMotion.value = e.matches\n  }\n\n  onMounted(() => {\n    if (typeof window === 'undefined' || typeof window.matchMedia !== 'function') return\n    mql = window.matchMedia(QUERY)\n    mql.addEventListener('change', handler)\n  })\n\n  onUnmounted(() => {\n    mql?.removeEventListener('change', handler)\n  })\n\n  return readonly(prefersReducedMotion)\n}\n```\n\n### Step 4.2: `src/composables/useToastTimer.ts`\n\nNew composable — extracts the pre-dismiss timer logic from React `GooeyToast.tsx` lines 818-862.\n\n```typescript\ninterface UseToastTimerOptions {\n  duration: number           // total display duration ms\n  expandDelayMs: number      // morph expand delay ms\n  collapseMs: number         // morph collapse duration ms\n  paused: Ref<boolean>       // containerHovered || toastHovered\n  enabled: Ref<boolean>      // showBody && !actionSuccess && !dismissing\n  onTimeout: () => void      // triggers pre-dismiss collapse\n}\n\ninterface UseToastTimerReturn {\n  progressDuration: Readonly<Ref<number>>  // for CSS progress bar\n  restart: () => void                       // reset timer on re-expand\n}\n```\n\nInternal state:\n- `remainingRef` — tracks remaining ms when paused\n- `timerStartRef` — timestamp when current timer started\n- Uses `watch()` on `paused` and `enabled` to start/pause/resume\n\n### Step 4.3: `src/composables/useSwipeToDismiss.ts`\n\nExtract swipe logic from React `GooeyToast.tsx` lines 1100-1154.\n\n```typescript\ninterface UseSwipeToDismissOptions {\n  enabled: () => boolean     // reads _config.swipeToDismiss\n  threshold?: number         // default 100\n  onDismiss: () => void\n}\n\ninterface UseSwipeToDismissReturn {\n  offsetX: Readonly<Ref<number>>\n  opacity: Readonly<Ref<number>>\n  swiping: Readonly<Ref<boolean>>\n  onPointerDown: (e: PointerEvent) => void\n  onPointerMove: (e: PointerEvent) => void\n  onPointerUp: () => void\n}\n```\n\nUses pointer events (not touch events) for broader compatibility.\n\n**Verification**: Unit tests for each composable (Phase 10).\n\n---\n\n## Phase 5: Simple Components\n\n### Step 5.1: Icons (`src/icons/`)\n\n6 Vue SFC files + `index.ts`. Each icon is a thin wrapper around the same SVG from React.\n\nExample — `SuccessIcon.vue`:\n```vue\n<script setup lang=\"ts\">\nwithDefaults(defineProps<{ size?: number; class?: string }>(), { size: 20 })\n</script>\n\n<template>\n  <svg\n    xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\"\n    :width=\"size\"\n    :height=\"size\"\n    viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n    fill=\"none\"\n    stroke=\"currentColor\"\n    stroke-width=\"2\"\n    stroke-linecap=\"round\"\n    stroke-linejoin=\"round\"\n    :class=\"$props.class\"\n  >\n    <circle cx=\"12\" cy=\"12\" r=\"10\" />\n    <path d=\"M9 12l2 2 4-4\" />\n  </svg>\n</template>\n```\n\nFiles to create:\n- `DefaultIcon.vue` — copy SVG paths from React `DefaultIcon.tsx`\n- `SuccessIcon.vue` — as above\n- `ErrorIcon.vue` — copy SVG paths from React `ErrorIcon.tsx`\n- `WarningIcon.vue` — copy SVG paths from React `WarningIcon.tsx`\n- `InfoIcon.vue` — copy SVG paths from React `InfoIcon.tsx`\n- `SpinnerIcon.vue` — copy SVG paths from React `SpinnerIcon.tsx`\n- `index.ts` — re-export all icons\n\n### Step 5.2: `src/components/AriaLiveAnnouncer.vue`\n\nPort from React `AriaLiveAnnouncer.tsx`. Uses `watch()` on `_announcement` from context.\n\n```vue\n<script setup lang=\"ts\">\nimport { ref, watch, nextTick } from 'vue'\nimport { _announcement, type Announcement } from '../context'\n\nconst politeMessage = ref('')\nconst assertiveMessage = ref('')\n\nwatch(_announcement, (ann: Announcement | null) => {\n  if (!ann) return\n  if (ann.politeness === 'assertive') {\n    assertiveMessage.value = ''\n    nextTick(() => { assertiveMessage.value = ann.message })\n  } else {\n    politeMessage.value = ''\n    nextTick(() => { politeMessage.value = ann.message })\n  }\n})\n\n// Clear after 7 seconds\nwatch(politeMessage, (msg) => {\n  if (!msg) return\n  const t = setTimeout(() => { politeMessage.value = '' }, 7000)\n  // Cleanup not needed for setTimeout in watch — component unmount handles it\n})\nwatch(assertiveMessage, (msg) => {\n  if (!msg) return\n  const t = setTimeout(() => { assertiveMessage.value = '' }, 7000)\n})\n</script>\n\n<template>\n  <div role=\"status\" aria-live=\"polite\" aria-atomic=\"true\"\n    :style=\"{ position: 'absolute', width: '1px', height: '1px', padding: 0, margin: '-1px', overflow: 'hidden', clip: 'rect(0,0,0,0)', whiteSpace: 'nowrap', border: 0 }\">\n    {{ politeMessage }}\n  </div>\n  <div role=\"alert\" aria-live=\"assertive\" aria-atomic=\"true\"\n    :style=\"{ position: 'absolute', width: '1px', height: '1px', padding: 0, margin: '-1px', overflow: 'hidden', clip: 'rect(0,0,0,0)', whiteSpace: 'nowrap', border: 0 }\">\n    {{ assertiveMessage }}\n  </div>\n</template>\n```\n\n### Step 5.3: `src/components/ToastErrorBoundary.vue`\n\nPort from React `ToastErrorBoundary.tsx`. Uses `onErrorCaptured()`.\n\n```vue\n<script setup lang=\"ts\">\nimport { ref, onErrorCaptured } from 'vue'\n\nconst hasError = ref(false)\n\nonErrorCaptured((err) => {\n  hasError.value = true\n  if (import.meta.env.DEV) {\n    console.error('[GooeyToast] Rendering error:', err)\n  }\n  return false // prevent propagation\n})\n</script>\n\n<template>\n  <slot v-if=\"!hasError\" />\n</template>\n```\n\nNote: React version renders `null` on error; Vue version renders nothing (empty template).\n\n**Verification**: Component mount tests in Phase 10.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 6: ToastContainer.vue\n\n**Goal**: New component that replaces Sonner's `<Toaster>`. Manages toast positioning, stacking, hover expansion.\n\n### Key responsibilities:\n1. `<Teleport to=\"body\">` — render fixed-position `<ol>` outside component tree\n2. Render `<li v-for=\"toast in _toasts\">` wrapping `<ToastErrorBoundary><GooeyToast /></ToastErrorBoundary>`\n3. Compute stacking offsets: each toast's `translateY` = sum of heights of all newer toasts + gaps\n4. Handle hover expand/collapse: `@mouseenter`/`@mouseleave` on `<ol>` sets `containerHovered`\n5. Stack scale effect: non-hovered toasts behind front get `scale(1 - i * 0.05)`\n\n### Props:\n```typescript\ninterface ToastContainerProps {\n  position: ToastPosition\n  gap: number\n  offset: number | string\n  theme: 'light' | 'dark'\n  dir: 'ltr' | 'rtl'\n}\n```\n\n### Implementation details:\n\n**Positioning**: CSS `position: fixed`. Position prop maps to CSS:\n- `bottom-right` → `bottom: {offset}; right: {offset}`\n- `top-center` → `top: {offset}; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%)`\n- etc.\n\n**Height tracking**: Each `<GooeyToast>` emits `heightChange(id, height)`. ToastContainer maintains a `Map<id, number>` of current heights. On change, recompute offsets for all toasts.\n\n**Stacking offsets**: `<li>` gets `style.transform = translate3d(0, ${offset}px, 0)`. For bottom positions, offset is negative (toasts stack upward). For top positions, offset is positive.\n\n**Hover behavior**:\n- `@mouseenter` on `<ol>`: set `containerHovered.value = true`, remove scale/translate compression\n- `@mouseleave` on `<ol>`: set `containerHovered.value = false`, apply scale/translate compression\n\n**Scale effect** (non-hovered):\n```\ntoast[0] (front/newest): scale(1), offset 0\ntoast[1]: scale(0.95), offset = height[0] + gap (compressed)\ntoast[2]: scale(0.90), offset = height[0] + height[1] + 2*gap (compressed)\n```\nWhen hovered: all toasts scale(1), offset = actual cumulative heights + gaps.\n\n**Transition**: `<li>` gets CSS `transition: transform 0.3s ease, opacity 0.3s ease`.\n\n**Toast removal**: When a toast is dismissed, it's removed from `_toasts` reactively. Vue's `<TransitionGroup>` or manual animation handles the exit.\n\n**Verification**: Stacking offset calculation tests + visual demo.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 7: GooeyToast.vue (Core Component)\n\n**Goal**: Port the 1350-line React `GooeyToast.tsx` to a Vue SFC.\n\nThis is the most complex step. The component manages:\n- SVG morph animation (pill → blob → pill)\n- Spring physics squish effects\n- State machine (mount → expand → display → pre-dismiss → collapse)\n- Error shake animation\n- Hover re-expand\n- Close button\n- Progress bar\n- Swipe-to-dismiss\n- Action button with success morph-back\n\n### Architecture decisions:\n\n1. **Animation controllers** stored as plain variables (not refs) — same as React's `useRef` pattern. Avoids triggering reactivity on animation internals.\n\n2. **`flush()` function** — direct DOM manipulation for SVG path and content constraints. Same approach as React — avoids reactive overhead on 60fps updates.\n\n3. **`measure()` function** — reads DOM dimensions. Called via `onMounted`, `ResizeObserver`, and `watch` on props that affect layout.\n\n4. **State machine** implemented via `watch()` on computed `isExpanded`:\n   - `isExpanded` true → delay → `showBody = true` → morph 0→1\n   - `isExpanded` false → morph 1→0 → `showBody = false`\n\n5. **Timer logic** → `useToastTimer` composable\n\n6. **Swipe logic** → `useSwipeToDismiss` composable\n\n### Props (mirrors React `GooeyToastProps`):\n\n```typescript\ninterface GooeyToastProps {\n  toast: InternalToast    // full toast data from gooey-toast.ts\n  toastId: string | number\n}\n```\n\n### Emits:\n\n```typescript\ndefineEmits<{\n  dismiss: [id: string | number]\n  heightChange: [id: string | number, height: number]\n}>()\n```\n\n### Key implementation mapping (React → Vue):\n\n| React | Vue |\n|---|---|\n| `useState(x)` | `ref(x)` |\n| `useRef(x)` | `let x = y` (non-reactive) or `ref(x)` if template needs it |\n| `useEffect(() => { ... }, [deps])` | `watch([deps], () => { ... })` or `watchEffect()` |\n| `useLayoutEffect` | `onMounted` + `watch({ flush: 'pre' })` |\n| `useCallback(fn, [deps])` | plain function (Vue doesn't re-create closures) |\n| `useMemo(fn, [deps])` | `computed(fn)` |\n| `AnimatePresence` + `motion.div` | `<Transition>` + manual opacity/transform |\n| `animate(from, to, opts)` | `animate(from, to, opts)` from `motion` package (same API) |\n| `sonnerToast.dismiss(id)` | `emit('dismiss', id)` |\n| `subscribeContainerHovered(cb)` | `watch(containerHovered, cb)` |\n\n### Section-by-section port:\n\n**7a. Template refs & non-reactive state:**\n- `wrapperRef`, `pathRef`, `headerRef`, `contentRef` → `useTemplateRef<HTMLDivElement>()` or `ref<HTMLElement | null>(null)`\n- `morphTRef`, `aDims`, `dimsRef`, `expandedDimsRef` → plain `let` variables (non-reactive, only used in imperative animation code)\n- Animation controllers (`morphCtrl`, `pillResizeCtrl`, etc.) → plain `let` variables\n\n**7b. Reactive state:**\n- `showBody`, `dismissing`, `hovered`, `actionSuccess`, `progressKey` → `ref()`\n- `dims` (triggers effects) → `ref({ pw: 0, bw: 0, th: 0 })`\n\n**7c. Computed:**\n- `effectiveTitle`, `effectivePhase`, `effectiveDescription`, `effectiveAction` → `computed()`\n- `isExpanded`, `isLoading`, `hasDescription`, `hasAction` → `computed()`\n- `isRight`, `isCenter` → `computed()` reading from `_config`\n- `useSpring`, `bounceVal`, `showProgress` → `computed()` merging props + presets + config\n\n**7d. `flush()` and `measure()`:**\nDirect copy from React. These are plain functions that read refs and write to DOM. No React/Vue dependency.\n\n**7e. Morph animation (Phase 2 in React):**\n`watch(showBody, (val) => { ... })` — drives morph 0→1 on true, 1→0 on false. Uses `animate()` from `motion` package.\n\n**7f. Pre-dismiss timer:**\nDelegate to `useToastTimer` composable.\n\n**7g. Re-expand on hover:**\n`watch([hovered, containerHovered], ...)` — if dismissing and now hovered, stop collapse, re-expand.\n\n**7h. Error shake:**\n`watch(() => props.toast.phase, (newPhase, oldPhase) => { ... })` — trigger shake animation on error transition.\n\n**7i. Landing squish:**\n`triggerLandingSquish()` — same imperative function, called from morph watchers.\n\n**7j. Header elastic squish:**\n`watch([showBody, dismissing, actionSuccess], ...)` — squish header during expand/collapse.\n\n**7k. Icon transition:**\nReplace `<AnimatePresence mode=\"wait\"><motion.div>` with `<Transition mode=\"out-in\">` using CSS opacity/scale.\n\n**7l. Body content (description, action, timestamp):**\nReplace `<AnimatePresence>` + `<motion.div>` with `<Transition>` components for fade-in/out.\n\n**7m. Dismiss from container:**\nInstead of `sonnerToast.dismiss(toastId)`, emit `dismiss` event. ToastContainer handles removal from `_toasts`.\n\n**7n. Height reporting:**\nOn mount and after dimension changes, emit `heightChange` so ToastContainer can update stacking.\n\n**Verification**: Component renders correctly, morph animations work, dismiss lifecycle completes.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 8: GooeyToaster.vue\n\n**Goal**: Top-level provider component. Syncs props to `_config`, renders `<ToastContainer>` + `<AriaLiveAnnouncer>`.\n\n### Props:\nAll `GooeyToasterProps` fields (same as React minus Sonner-specific ones).\n\n### Implementation:\n\n```vue\n<script setup lang=\"ts\">\nimport { watch, onMounted, onUnmounted } from 'vue'\nimport { _config } from '../context'\nimport { gooeyToast, _getMostRecentActiveId } from '../gooey-toast'\nimport { animationPresets } from '../presets'\nimport type { GooeyToasterProps } from '../types'\nimport ToastContainer from './ToastContainer.vue'\nimport AriaLiveAnnouncer from './AriaLiveAnnouncer.vue'\n\nconst props = withDefaults(defineProps<GooeyToasterProps>(), {\n  position: 'bottom-right',\n  gap: 14,\n  offset: '24px',\n  theme: 'light',\n  visibleToasts: 3,\n  swipeToDismiss: true,\n  closeOnEscape: true,\n  maxQueue: Infinity,\n  queueOverflow: 'drop-oldest',\n  showProgress: false,\n  closeButton: false,\n  dir: 'ltr',\n})\n\n// Sync all props to reactive config\nwatch(() => props.position, (v) => { _config.position = v }, { immediate: true })\nwatch(() => props.dir, (v) => { _config.dir = v ?? 'ltr' }, { immediate: true })\nwatch(() => props.theme, (v) => { _config.theme = v }, { immediate: true })\n// ... etc for all config fields\n\n// Resolve spring/bounce from preset\nconst resolvedSpring = computed(() => props.spring ?? (props.preset ? animationPresets[props.preset]?.spring : undefined) ?? true)\nconst resolvedBounce = computed(() => props.bounce ?? (props.preset ? animationPresets[props.preset]?.bounce : undefined))\n\nwatch(resolvedSpring, (v) => { _config.spring = v }, { immediate: true })\nwatch(resolvedBounce, (v) => { _config.bounce = v }, { immediate: true })\n\n// Escape key handler\nonMounted(() => {\n  const handler = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {\n    if (!_config.closeOnEscape) return\n    if (e.key === 'Escape') {\n      const id = _getMostRecentActiveId()\n      if (id != null) gooeyToast.dismiss(id)\n    }\n  }\n  document.addEventListener('keydown', handler)\n  onUnmounted(() => document.removeEventListener('keydown', handler))\n})\n\n// CSS detection warning (dev only)\nonMounted(() => {\n  if (import.meta.env.DEV) {\n    const el = document.createElement('div')\n    el.setAttribute('data-gooey-toast-css', '')\n    el.style.cssText = 'position:absolute;width:0;height:0;overflow:hidden;pointer-events:none'\n    document.body.appendChild(el)\n    const value = getComputedStyle(el).getPropertyValue('--gooey-toast')\n    document.body.removeChild(el)\n    if (!value) {\n      console.warn('[gooey-toast] Styles not found. Import CSS:\\n\\n  import \"goey-toast-vue/styles.css\";\\n')\n    }\n  }\n})\n</script>\n\n<template>\n  <ToastContainer\n    :position=\"props.position\"\n    :gap=\"props.gap\"\n    :offset=\"props.offset\"\n    :theme=\"props.theme\"\n    :dir=\"props.dir ?? 'ltr'\"\n  />\n  <AriaLiveAnnouncer />\n</template>\n```\n\n**Verification**: Props sync to `_config`, Escape key dismisses toasts, CSS detection works.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 9: Public API + CSS\n\n### Step 9.1: `src/index.ts`\n\n```typescript\nimport './components/GooeyToast.css'\n\nexport { default as GooeyToaster } from './components/GooeyToaster.vue'\nexport { gooeyToast } from './gooey-toast'\nexport { animationPresets } from './presets'\nexport type { AnimationPreset, AnimationPresetName } from './presets'\nexport type {\n  GooeyToastOptions,\n  GooeyPromiseData,\n  GooeyToasterProps,\n  GooeyToastAction,\n  GooeyToastClassNames,\n  GooeyToastTimings,\n  GooeyToastUpdateOptions,\n  DismissFilter,\n  ToastPosition,\n} from './types'\n\n// Backward-compatible aliases\nexport { default as GoeyToaster } from './components/GooeyToaster.vue'\nexport { gooeyToast as goeyToast } from './gooey-toast'\n```\n\n### Step 9.2: `src/components/GooeyToast.css`\n\nCopy from React `GooeyToast.css`. Changes:\n- **Remove** all Sonner-specific selectors:\n  - `[data-sonner-toast]` rules (lines 12-18, 37-39)\n  - `[data-sonner-toaster]` rules (lines 21-27)\n  - `[data-x-position=\"center\"]` rules\n  - `[data-expanded=\"true\"]` transition override\n- **Add** ToastContainer positioning styles:\n\n```css\n/* Toast container — fixed position overlay */\n.gooey-toast-container {\n  position: fixed;\n  z-index: 999999;\n  pointer-events: none;\n  list-style: none;\n  margin: 0;\n  padding: 0;\n}\n\n.gooey-toast-container > li {\n  pointer-events: auto;\n  transition: transform 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1),\n              opacity 0.3s ease;\n}\n```\n\n- **Keep** everything else unchanged (wrapper, content, header, title colors, description, action buttons, progress bar, dark mode, close button, spinner)\n\n**Verification**: CSS loads with `--gooey-toast: 1` detection marker intact.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 10: Tests\n\n### Test file mapping (React → Vue):\n\n| React test | Vue test | Notes |\n|---|---|---|\n| `gooey-toast.test.tsx` | `gooey-toast.test.ts` | No JSX — tests pure functions + reactive state |\n| `GooeyToast.test.tsx` | `GooeyToast.test.ts` | Uses `@vue/test-utils` mount |\n| `GooeyToaster.test.tsx` | `GooeyToaster.test.ts` | Uses `@vue/test-utils` mount |\n| `AriaLiveAnnouncer.test.tsx` | `AriaLiveAnnouncer.test.ts` | Watch-based |\n| `ToastErrorBoundary.test.tsx` | `ToastErrorBoundary.test.ts` | onErrorCaptured |\n| `usePrefersReducedMotion.test.tsx` | `usePrefersReducedMotion.test.ts` | Composable test |\n| — | `morph.test.ts` | **New** — pure function tests |\n| — | `spring.test.ts` | **New** — pure function tests |\n| — | `ToastContainer.test.ts` | **New** — stacking, positioning |\n\n### Test approach:\n\n**Pure function tests** (`morph.test.ts`, `spring.test.ts`):\n- Test morph path at t=0 (pill), t=1 (full blob), intermediate values\n- Test center morph path symmetry\n- Test memoizePath caching\n- Test squishSpring parameter ranges\n- Snapshot SVG path strings for regression\n\n**State management tests** (`gooey-toast.test.ts`):\n- `createGooeyToast()` adds to `_toasts`\n- Queue: 4th toast goes to queue when `visibleToasts=3`\n- `dismissGooeyToast(id)` removes by ID\n- `dismissGooeyToast({ type: 'error' })` removes by type filter\n- `dismissGooeyToast()` clears all\n- `updateGooeyToast()` mutates in-place\n- Promise toast: loading → success transition\n- Promise toast: loading → error transition\n- Callbacks: `onDismiss` called on dismiss\n- Callbacks: `onAutoClose` called on auto-dismiss, NOT on manual dismiss\n\n**Component tests** (using `@vue/test-utils`):\n- Mock `motion`'s `animate()` to return `{ stop: () => {} }`\n- Mount with minimal props, verify renders\n- Verify ARIA roles (status vs alert based on type)\n- Verify description renders when provided\n- Verify action button triggers callback\n\n**Verification**: `npm run test` passes all tests.\n\n---\n\n## Phase 11: Build & Verify\n\n### Step 11.1: Build\n\n```bash\ncd goey-toast-vue && npm run build\n```\n\nVerify output:\n- `dist/index.js` (ESM)\n- `dist/index.cjs` (CJS)\n- `dist/index.d.ts` (types)\n- `dist/style.css` (styles)\n\n### Step 11.2: Type check\n\n```bash\nnpm run typecheck\n```\n\nMust exit 0 with no errors.\n\n### Step 11.3: Tests\n\n```bash\nnpm run test\n```\n\nAll tests pass.\n\n### Step 11.4: Demo app (optional)\n\nCreate `goey-toast-vue/demo/` with a minimal Vite + Vue 3 app that imports the library and shows all toast types. This is for manual visual verification.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Implementation Notes\n\n### What can be directly copied (zero changes):\n- `presets.ts`\n- `gooey-styles.ts`\n- SVG morph math (`morphPathRaw`, `morphPathCenterRaw`)\n- Spring physics (`squishSpring`)\n- `SMOOTH_EASE`, `PH`, `DEFAULT_DISPLAY_DURATION`\n- Icon SVG paths\n- All CSS class names and most CSS rules\n- `buildAnnouncementMessage()`, `getAnnouncePoliteness()`\n\n### What requires architectural rewrite:\n- `gooey-toast.ts` — completely different lifecycle (no Sonner, no React components)\n- `ToastContainer.vue` — entirely new (replaces Sonner)\n- `GooeyToast.vue` — same logic, different framework primitives\n- `GooeyToaster.vue` — much simpler (no Sonner `<Toaster>`, no MutationObserver hacks)\n- `context.ts` — reactive() replaces getter/setter/pub-sub pattern\n\n### What is eliminated entirely:\n- All `syncSonnerHeights()` code — not needed without Sonner\n- `registerSonnerObserver()` — not needed without Sonner\n- `GooeyToastWrapper` / `PromiseToastWrapper` React components — replaced by reactive data\n- React StrictMode double-mount guards\n- Sonner's `toast.custom()` / `toast.dismiss()` calls\n- `toastOptions`, `richColors`, `expand` props (Sonner-specific)\n\n### Risk areas:\n1. **Animation timing parity** — Motion's `animate()` API should be identical to Framer Motion's standalone version, but verify spring parameter interpretation matches.\n2. **ToastContainer stacking** — The most novel code (replaces Sonner). Needs careful height tracking and offset calculation.\n3. **GooeyToast.vue complexity** — 1350 lines of animation logic. Port section by section, verify each animation works before moving to the next.\n4. **Icon cross-fade** — `<Transition mode=\"out-in\">` may not perfectly match `<AnimatePresence mode=\"wait\">` timing. May need manual transition hooks.\n"
}
```

```
Wrote file successfully.

3. **State management** — context.ts (reactive singleton), gooey-toast.ts (queue + API)
4. **Composables** — usePrefersReducedMotion, useToastTimer, useSwipeToDismiss

6. **ToastContainer** — new component replacing Sonner (positioning, stacking, hover)
7. **GooeyToast.vue** — core 1350-line component port (morph, springs, state machine)
8. **GooeyToaster.vue** — top-level provider
9. **Public API + CSS** — index.ts exports, CSS adaptation

<auto-slash-command>
# /handoff Command

**Description**: (builtin) Create a detailed context summary for continuing work in a new session

**Scope**: builtin

---

## Command Instructions

<command-instruction>
# Handoff Command

## Purpose

Use /handoff when:
- The current session context is getting too long and quality is degrading
- You want to start fresh while preserving essential context from this session
- The context window is approaching capacity

This creates a detailed context summary that can be used to continue work in a new session.

---

# PHASE 0: VALIDATE REQUEST

Before proceeding, confirm:
- [ ] There is meaningful work or context in this session to preserve
- [ ] The user wants to create a handoff summary (not just asking about it)

If the session is nearly empty or has no meaningful context, inform the user there is nothing substantial to hand off.

---

# PHASE 1: GATHER PROGRAMMATIC CONTEXT

Execute these tools to gather concrete data:

1. session_read({ session_id: "$SESSION_ID" }) - full session history
2. todoread() - current task progress
3. Bash({ command: "git diff --stat HEAD~10..HEAD" }) - recent file changes
4. Bash({ command: "git status --porcelain" }) - uncommitted changes

Suggested execution order:

```
session_read({ session_id: "$SESSION_ID" })
todoread()
Bash({ command: "git diff --stat HEAD~10..HEAD" })
Bash({ command: "git status --porcelain" })
```

Analyze the gathered outputs to understand:
- What the user asked for (exact wording)
- What work was completed
- What tasks remain incomplete (include todo state)
- What decisions were made
- What files were modified or discussed (include git diff/stat + status)
- What patterns, constraints, or preferences were established

---

# PHASE 2: EXTRACT CONTEXT

Write the context summary from first person perspective ("I did...", "I told you...").

Focus on:
- Capabilities and behavior, not file-by-file implementation details
- What matters for continuing the work
- Avoiding excessive implementation details (variable names, storage keys, constants) unless critical
- USER REQUESTS (AS-IS) must be verbatim (do not paraphrase)
- EXPLICIT CONSTRAINTS must be verbatim only (do not invent)

Questions to consider when extracting:
- What did I just do or implement?
- What instructions did I already give which are still relevant (e.g. follow patterns in the codebase)?
- What files did I tell you are important or that I am working on?
- Did I provide a plan or spec that should be included?
- What did I already tell you that is important (libraries, patterns, constraints, preferences)?
- What important technical details did I discover (APIs, methods, patterns)?
- What caveats, limitations, or open questions did I find?

---

# PHASE 3: FORMAT OUTPUT

Generate a handoff summary using this exact format:

```
HANDOFF CONTEXT
===============

USER REQUESTS (AS-IS)
---------------------
- [Exact verbatim user requests - NOT paraphrased]

GOAL
----
[One sentence describing what should be done next]

WORK COMPLETED
--------------
- [First person bullet points of what was done]
- [Include specific file paths when relevant]
- [Note key implementation decisions]

CURRENT STATE
-------------
- [Current state of the codebase or task]
- [Build/test status if applicable]
- [Any environment or configuration state]

PENDING TASKS
-------------
- [Tasks that were planned but not completed]
- [Next logical steps to take]
- [Any blockers or issues encountered]
- [Include current todo state from todoread()]

KEY FILES
---------
- [path/to/file1] - [brief role description]
- [path/to/file2] - [brief role description]
(Maximum 10 files, prioritized by importance)
- (Include files from git diff/stat and git status)

IMPORTANT DECISIONS
-------------------
- [Technical decisions that were made and why]
- [Trade-offs that were considered]
- [Patterns or conventions established]

EXPLICIT CONSTRAINTS
--------------------
- [Verbatim constraints only - from user or existing AGENTS.md]
- If none, write: None

CONTEXT FOR CONTINUATION
------------------------
- [What the next session needs to know to continue]
- [Warnings or gotchas to be aware of]
- [References to documentation if relevant]
```

Rules for the summary:
- Plain text with bullets
- No markdown headers with # (use the format above with dashes)
- No bold, italic, or code fences within content
- Use workspace-relative paths for files
- Keep it focused - only include what matters for continuation
- Pick an appropriate length based on complexity
- USER REQUESTS (AS-IS) and EXPLICIT CONSTRAINTS must be verbatim only

---

# PHASE 4: PROVIDE INSTRUCTIONS

After generating the summary, instruct the user:

```
---

TO CONTINUE IN A NEW SESSION:

1. Press 'n' in OpenCode TUI to open a new session, or run 'opencode' in a new terminal
2. Paste the HANDOFF CONTEXT above as your first message
3. Add your request: "Continue from the handoff context above. [Your next task]"

The new session will have all context needed to continue seamlessly.
```

---

# IMPORTANT CONSTRAINTS

- DO NOT attempt to programmatically create new sessions (no API available to agents)
- DO provide a self-contained summary that works without access to this session
- DO include workspace-relative file paths
- DO NOT include sensitive information (API keys, credentials, secrets)
- DO NOT exceed 10 files in the KEY FILES section
- DO keep the GOAL section to a single sentence or short paragraph

---

# EXECUTE NOW

Begin by gathering programmatic context, then synthesize the handoff summary.

</command-instruction>

<session-context>
Session ID: $SESSION_ID
Timestamp: $TIMESTAMP
</session-context>

<user-request>

</user-request>

PENDING TASKS
-------------

- Phase 2: Port pure functions and types (types.ts, presets.ts, morph.ts, spring.ts, gooey-styles.ts)

KEY FILES
---------
- docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-goey-toast-vue.md - Implementation plan (1081 lines, THE execution guide)
- docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-17-goey-toast-vue-design.md - Design spec (297 lines, reference for decisions)
- goey-toast-react/src/components/GooeyToast.tsx - Core React component (1350 lines, primary port target)
- goey-toast-react/src/gooey-toast.tsx - Toast API + queue management (502 lines, needs architectural rewrite)
- goey-toast-react/src/components/GooeyToaster.tsx - React top-level provider (192 lines, Sonner wrapper)
- goey-toast-react/src/context.ts - Global config getter/setter + pub/sub (163 lines, becomes reactive)
- goey-toast-react/src/types.ts - TypeScript types (126 lines, ReactNode -> VNode | string)
- goey-toast-react/src/components/GooeyToast.css - Styles (367 lines, mostly direct copy)
- goey-toast-react/src/__tests__/gooey-toast.test.tsx - Main test file (558 lines, reference for test patterns)
- goey-toast-react/package.json - React version deps/scripts (reference for Vue version)

IMPORTANT DECISIONS
-------------------
- Animation engine: Motion (motion.dev) - Framer Motion's framework-agnostic version, animate() API is nearly identical
- Toast container: Built from scratch replacing Sonner - React version heavily hacks Sonner, self-implementation is cleaner
- Vue version: Only Vue 3 (>=3.3) using Composition API, Teleport, defineProps generics
- Architecture: Vue idiomatic rewrite - internal uses Vue reactive system, external API matches React exactly
- State management: Vue reactive() singleton replaces getter/setter + pub/sub pattern; no subscribe functions needed
- Toast lifecycle: Reactive array _toasts replaces Sonner's toast.custom()/toast.dismiss() calls
- GooeyToastWrapper/PromiseToastWrapper React components eliminated - ToastContainer renders directly from reactive data
- All syncSonnerHeights/registerSonnerObserver code eliminated (Sonner-specific hacks)
- React StrictMode double-mount guards eliminated
- Build: Vite lib mode + @vitejs/plugin-vue + vite-plugin-dts (not tsup, because SFC needs vue plugin)
- About 40% of code can be directly copied (pure functions, CSS, class name map), 60% needs rewrite

EXPLICIT CONSTRAINTS
--------------------
- User chose "Motion (推荐)" as animation engine
- User chose "从零实现（推荐）" to replace Sonner
- User chose "仅 Vue 3（推荐）"
- User chose "100% 功能对等（推荐）"
- User chose "API 对齐 React 版（推荐）"
- User chose "方案 A: Vue 惯用法重写（推荐）"
- User chose "在顶层初始化 git 仓库"

CONTEXT FOR CONTINUATION
------------------------

- The design spec at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-17-goey-toast-vue-design.md has the approved architecture and component hierarchy.
- All React source files under goey-toast-react/src/ must be read fresh in the new session as reference during implementation.
- The plan is organized in strict dependency order (Phase 1 before 2, etc.). Phases 2-5 have internal parallelism potential.
- Risk areas identified: (1) Motion animate() spring parameter parity with Framer Motion, (2) ToastContainer stacking is novel code, (3) GooeyToast.vue is 1350 lines of animation logic requiring section-by-section port.
- The plan includes specific code snippets and type signatures for most files - use them as starting points, not as copy-paste targets.
- subagent-driven-development skill is recommended for execution (independent phases can run in parallel).

## Assistant (Compaction · Claude Opus 4.6 · 35.0s)



## Goal

Port goey-toast-react library to goey-toast-vue (Vue 3) with 100% feature parity, consistent public API, and Vue-idiomatic internal implementation. The project spans three sessions: brainstorming (session 1), design spec + planning (sessions 2-3), and implementation (next).

## Instructions

- Read and follow the implementation plan at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-goey-toast-vue.md` — it is the authoritative execution guide with 11 phases
- The design spec at `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-17-goey-toast-vue-design.md` contains all approved architectural decisions
- User chose: Motion (motion.dev) as animation engine, from-scratch toast container (no Sonner), Vue 3 only (>=3.3), 100% feature parity, API aligned with React version, Vue idiomatic rewrite (方案 A)
- About 40% of code can be directly copied (pure functions, CSS, class name maps); 60% needs rewrite
- Key React→Vue mappings: `useState` → `ref()`, `useEffect` → `watch()`, `useCallback` → plain function, `useMemo` → `computed()`, `AnimatePresence` → `<Transition>`, Sonner's `toast.custom()/toast.dismiss()` → reactive `_toasts` array, getter/setter + pub/sub → `reactive()` singleton
- Build system: Vite lib mode + @vitejs/plugin-vue + vite-plugin-dts (not tsup, because SFC needs vue plugin)

## Discoveries

- React version heavily hacks Sonner (syncSonnerHeights, registerSonnerObserver, MutationObserver tricks) — all eliminated in Vue port since we build our own ToastContainer
- React StrictMode double-mount guards (mountedRef + setTimeout pattern) not needed in Vue
- GooeyToastWrapper and PromiseToastWrapper React components are eliminated — ToastContainer renders directly from reactive toast data
- Motion's `animate()` API is nearly identical to Framer Motion's standalone version, making animation code portable
- The core GooeyToast.tsx is 1350 lines with complex animation state machine: mount → expand delay (330ms) → showBody=true → morphT 0→1 → display timer → pre-dismiss → morphT 1→0 → emit dismiss
- SVG morph path generation (morphPathRaw, morphPathCenterRaw) and spring physics (squishSpring) are pure functions — direct copy with zero changes
- Risk areas: (1) Motion animate() spring parameter interpretation parity, (2) ToastContainer stacking is novel code replacing Sonner, (3) GooeyToast.vue is 1350 lines needing section-by-section port

## Accomplished

**Completed (across 3 sessions):**
- ✅ Brainstorming with user — all clarifying questions answered, all design choices confirmed
- ✅ Read ALL React source files in full detail (types.ts, context.ts, presets.ts, gooey-toast.tsx, GooeyToast.tsx 1350 lines, GooeyToaster.tsx, GooeyToast.css, gooey-styles.ts, AriaLiveAnnouncer.tsx, ToastErrorBoundary.tsx, all 6 icon files, all 7 test files, package.json, index.ts)
- ✅ Design spec written and committed: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-17-goey-toast-vue-design.md` (297 lines, commit d90afe2)
- ✅ Implementation plan written and committed: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-goey-toast-vue.md` (1081 lines, commit 4788e92)
- ✅ Plan self-reviewed against design spec — full coverage confirmed
- ✅ Git repo initialized at project root with 2 commits

**Not started — all 11 implementation phases:**
- Phase 1: Scaffolding (package.json, tsconfig, vite.config, vitest.config, env.d.ts)
- Phase 2: Pure functions & types (types.ts, presets.ts, morph.ts, spring.ts, gooey-styles.ts)
- Phase 3: State management (context.ts with reactive, gooey-toast.ts with reactive array)
- Phase 4: Composables (usePrefersReducedMotion, useToastTimer, useSwipeToDismiss)

205: ## Phase 2: Pure Functions & Types
206: 
207: **Goal**: Port all framework-agnostic code. These files have zero Vue/React imports.
208: 
209: ### Step 2.1: `src/types.ts`
210: 
211: Copy from React `types.ts`. Changes:
212: - Remove `import type { ReactNode } from 'react'`
213: - Remove `import type { ExternalToast, ToasterProps } from 'sonner'`
214: - Replace all `ReactNode` with `VNode | string` (import from `vue`)
215: - Replace `ToasterProps['position']` with inline union type `'top-left' | 'top-center' | 'top-right' | 'bottom-left' | 'bottom-center' | 'bottom-right'`
216: - Remove `GooeyToasterProps.toastOptions` (Sonner-specific `Partial<ExternalToast>`)
217: - Remove `GooeyToasterProps.richColors` (Sonner-specific)
218: - Remove `GooeyToasterProps.expand` (Sonner-specific — our ToastContainer handles this)
219: - Keep `GooeyToasterProps.visibleToasts` (we implement this ourselves)
220: 
221: Full type list to port:
222: - `GooeyToastType` — identical
223: - `GooeyToastTimings` — identical
224: - `GooeyToastClassNames` — identical
225: - `GooeyToastAction` — identical
226: - `GooeyToastData` — `ReactNode` → `VNode | string`
227: - `GooeyToastOptions` — `ReactNode` → `VNode | string`
228: - `GooeyPromiseData<T>` — `ReactNode` → `VNode | string`
229: - `GooeyToastPhase` — identical
230: - `GooeyToastUpdateOptions` — `ReactNode` → `VNode | string`
231: - `DismissFilter` — identical
232: - `GooeyToasterProps` — remove Sonner fields, inline position type
233: - `ToastPosition` — new exported type alias for the 6-position union
234: 
235: ### Step 2.2: `src/presets.ts`
236: 
237: **Direct copy** from React `presets.ts`. Zero changes needed.
238: 
239: ```typescript
240: export interface AnimationPreset {
241:   bounce: number
242:   spring: boolean
243: }
244: 
245: export const animationPresets = {
246:   smooth: { bounce: 0.1, spring: true },
247:   bouncy: { bounce: 0.6, spring: true },
248:   subtle: { bounce: 0.05, spring: true },
249:   snappy: { bounce: 0.4, spring: true },
250: } as const satisfies Record<string, AnimationPreset>
251: 
252: export type AnimationPresetName = keyof typeof animationPresets
253: ```
254: 
255: ### Step 2.3: `src/morph.ts`
256: 
257: Extract from React `GooeyToast.tsx` lines 221-360. Three exports:
258: - `memoizePath(fn)` — generic last-result cache
259: - `morphPath` — memoized `morphPathRaw`
260: - `morphPathCenter` — memoized `morphPathCenterRaw`
261: - `PH` constant (= 34)
262: 
263: Also export the raw functions for testing:
264: - `morphPathRaw(pw, bw, th, t)`
265: - `morphPathCenterRaw(pw, bw, th, t)`
266: 
267: **Zero changes** to the math. Direct copy.
268:

270: 
271: Extract from React `GooeyToast.tsx` lines 77-86. Exports:
272: - `squishSpring(durationSec, defaultDur, bounce?)` — returns `{ type: 'spring', stiffness, damping, mass }`
273: - `DEFAULT_EXPAND_DUR` (= 0.6)
274: - `DEFAULT_COLLAPSE_DUR` (= 0.9)
275: - `SMOOTH_EASE` (= [0.4, 0, 0.2, 1])
276: 
277: **Zero changes**. Direct copy.
278: 
279: ### Step 2.5: `src/components/gooey-styles.ts`
280: 
281: **Direct copy** from React `gooey-styles.ts`. Zero changes.
282: 
283: **Verification**: `npm run typecheck` passes for Phase 2 files.

285: ---
286: 
287: ## Phase 3: State Management
288: 
289: **Goal**: Replace React's module-level variables + pub/sub with Vue reactive primitives.
290: 
291: ### Step 3.1: `src/context.ts`
292: 
293: Replace the React version's getter/setter + pub/sub pattern with `reactive`/`ref`:
294: 
295: ```typescript
296: import { reactive, ref } from 'vue'
297: import type { ToastPosition } from './types'
298: 
299: // Global config — reactive singleton
300: export const _config = reactive({
301:   position: 'bottom-right' as ToastPosition,
302:   dir: 'ltr' as 'ltr' | 'rtl',
303:   spring: true,
304:   bounce: undefined as number | undefined,
305:   theme: 'light' as 'light' | 'dark',
306:   visibleToasts: 3,
307:   swipeToDismiss: true,
308:   closeOnEscape: true,
309:   maxQueue: Infinity,
310:   queueOverflow: 'drop-oldest' as 'drop-oldest' | 'drop-newest',
311:   showProgress: false,
312:   closeButton: false as boolean | 'top-left' | 'top-right',
313: })
314: 
315: // Container hover state — watched by GooeyToast instances
316: export const containerHovered = ref(false)
317: 
318: // ARIA announcements — watched by AriaLiveAnnouncer
319: export type AriaLivePoliteness = 'polite' | 'assertive'
320: export interface Announcement {
321:   message: string
322:   politeness: AriaLivePoliteness
323: }
324: export const _announcement = ref<Announcement | null>(null)
325: 
326: export function announce(message: string, politeness: AriaLivePoliteness = 'polite') {
327:   // Set to null first to trigger watchers even for identical messages
328:   _announcement.value = null
329:   // nextTick or queueMicrotask to ensure the null is observed before the new value
330:   queueMicrotask(() => {
331:     _announcement.value = { message, politeness }
332:   })
333: }
334: ```
335: 
336: Key difference from React version:
337: - No getter/setter functions needed — direct property access on `_config`
338: - No `subscribeContainerHovered()` — components `watch(containerHovered, ...)`
339: - No `subscribeAnnouncements()` — AriaLiveAnnouncer `watch(_announcement, ...)`
340: - `announce()` uses null→value pattern to re-trigger watchers for duplicate messages
341: 
342: ### Step 3.2: `src/gooey-toast.ts`
343: 
344: The most complex state file. Port from React `gooey-toast.tsx` (502 lines).
345: 
346: Key architectural changes:
347: 1. **No Sonner dependency** — `toast.custom()` / `toast.dismiss()` replaced by reactive array
348: 2. **No React components** — `GooeyToastWrapper` / `PromiseToastWrapper` are not needed; ToastContainer renders directly from the reactive toast list
349: 3. **Toast lifecycle managed by reactive data**, not Sonner unmount
350: 
351: Data structures:
352: 
353: ```typescript
354: import { ref, type Ref } from 'vue'
355: 
356: export interface InternalToast {
357:   id: string | number
358:   title: string
359:   type: GooeyToastType
360:   phase: GooeyToastPhase
361:   description?: VNode | string
362:   action?: GooeyToastAction
363:   icon?: VNode | string
364:   duration?: number
365:   classNames?: GooeyToastClassNames
366:   fillColor?: string
367:   borderColor?: string
368:   borderWidth?: number
369:   timing?: GooeyToastTimings
370:   preset?: AnimationPresetName
371:   spring?: boolean
372:   bounce?: number
373:   showProgress?: boolean
374:   showTimestamp?: boolean
375:   // Promise-specific
376:   _promise?: Promise<unknown>
377:   _promiseData?: GooeyPromiseData<unknown>
378:   // Callbacks
379:   _onDismiss?: (id: string | number) => void
380:   _onAutoClose?: (id: string | number) => void
381: }
382: 
383: // Active toasts visible on screen
384: export const _toasts: Ref<InternalToast[]> = ref([])
385: 
386: // Queue for overflow
387: const _queue: Array<{ id: string | number; type: GooeyToastType; toast: InternalToast }> = []
388: 
389: // Track manual vs auto dismiss
390: const _autoCloseFlags = new Set<string | number>()
391: const _manualDismissFlags = new Set<string | number>()
392: ```
393: 
394: Functions to port (with changes noted):
395: 
396: - `createGooeyToast(title, type, options)` — Instead of `toast.custom()`, push to `_toasts.value`. Queue logic same as React.
397: - `dismissGooeyToast(idOrFilter?)` — Instead of `toast.dismiss()`, splice from `_toasts.value`. Type-filter and dismiss-all logic same as React.
398: - `updateGooeyToast(id, options)` — Find toast in `_toasts.value` by id, mutate fields directly (Vue reactivity picks up changes). No listener registry needed.
399: - `_processQueue()` — Same logic, but `create` callback pushes to `_toasts.value`.
400: - `_enqueue(entry)` — Same logic.
401: - `_onToastDismissed(id)` — Remove from `_toasts.value`, invoke callbacks, process queue.
402: - `_markAutoClose(id)` — Same.
403: - `_resetQueue()` — Same (clear `_toasts.value`, `_queue`, flags).
404: - `_getMostRecentActiveId()` — Return `_toasts.value[_toasts.value.length - 1]?.id`.
405: 
406: Promise toast handling:
407: - Instead of `PromiseToastWrapper` component, the `gooeyToast.promise()` function creates an `InternalToast` with `phase: 'loading'` and `_promise` / `_promiseData` fields.
408: - A `promise.then().catch()` chain updates the toast in `_toasts.value` when it resolves/rejects — mutating `title`, `phase`, `description`, `action` directly.
409: 
410: ARIA announcements:
411: - Call `announce()` from `context.ts` in `createGooeyToast()` and `updateGooeyToast()`.
412: - `buildAnnouncementMessage()` and `getAnnouncePoliteness()` — direct copy.
413: 
414: Public API shape — identical to React:
415: 
416: ```typescript
417: export const gooeyToast = Object.assign(
418:   (title: string, options?: GooeyToastOptions) => createGooeyToast(title, 'default', options),
419:   {
420:     success: (title, options?) => createGooeyToast(title, 'success', options),
421:     error: (title, options?) => createGooeyToast(title, 'error', options),
422:     warning: (title, options?) => createGooeyToast(title, 'warning', options),
423:     info: (title, options?) => createGooeyToast(title, 'info', options),
424:     promise: <T,>(promise: Promise<T>, data: GooeyPromiseData<T>) => { ... },
425:     dismiss: dismissGooeyToast,
426:     update: updateGooeyToast,
427:   }
428: )
429: ```
430: 
431: **Verification**: Unit tests for queue logic, dismiss filters, promise state transitions (Phase 10).
432: 
433: ---
434: 
435: ## Phase 4: Composables
436: 
437: ### Step 4.1: `src/composables/usePrefersReducedMotion.ts`
438: 
439: Port from React `usePrefersReducedMotion.ts`:
440: 
441: ```typescript
442: import { ref, onMounted, onUnmounted, readonly, type DeepReadonly, type Ref } from 'vue'
443: 
444: const QUERY = '(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)'
445: 
446: export function usePrefersReducedMotion(): Readonly<Ref<boolean>> {
447:   const prefersReducedMotion = ref(
448:     typeof window !== 'undefined' && typeof window.matchMedia === 'function'
449:       ? window.matchMedia(QUERY).matches
450:       : false
451:   )
452: 
453:   let mql: MediaQueryList | null = null
454:   const handler = (e: MediaQueryListEvent) => {
455:     prefersReducedMotion.value = e.matches
456:   }
457: 
458:   onMounted(() => {
459:     if (typeof window === 'undefined' || typeof window.matchMedia !== 'function') return
460:     mql = window.matchMedia(QUERY)
461:     mql.addEventListener('change', handler)
462:   })
463: 
464:   onUnmounted(() => {
465:     mql?.removeEventListener('change', handler)
466:   })
467: 
468:   return readonly(prefersReducedMotion)
469: }
470: ```
471: 
472: ### Step 4.2: `src/composables/useToastTimer.ts`
473: 
474: New composable — extracts the pre-dismiss timer logic from React `GooeyToast.tsx` lines 818-862.
475: 
476: ```typescript
477: interface UseToastTimerOptions {
478:   duration: number           // total display duration ms
479:   expandDelayMs: number      // morph expand delay ms
480:   collapseMs: number         // morph collapse duration ms
481:   paused: Ref<boolean>       // containerHovered || toastHovered
482:   enabled: Ref<boolean>      // showBody && !actionSuccess && !dismissing
483:   onTimeout: () => void      // triggers pre-dismiss collapse
484: }
485: 
486: interface UseToastTimerReturn {
487:   progressDuration: Readonly<Ref<number>>  // for CSS progress bar
488:   restart: () => void                       // reset timer on re-expand
489: }
490: ```
491: 
492: Internal state:
493: - `remainingRef` — tracks remaining ms when paused
494: - `timerStartRef` — timestamp when current timer started
495: - Uses `watch()` on `paused` and `enabled` to start/pause/resume
496: 
497: ### Step 4.3: `src/composables/useSwipeToDismiss.ts`
498: 
499: Extract swipe logic from React `GooeyToast.tsx` lines 1100-1154.
500: 
501: ```typescript
502: interface UseSwipeToDismissOptions {
503:   enabled: () => boolean     // reads _config.swipeToDismiss
504:   threshold?: number         // default 100
505:   onDismiss: () => void
506: }
507: 
508: interface UseSwipeToDismissReturn {
509:   offsetX: Readonly<Ref<number>>
510:   opacity: Readonly<Ref<number>>
511:   swiping: Readonly<Ref<boolean>>
512:   onPointerDown: (e: PointerEvent) => void
513:   onPointerMove: (e: PointerEvent) => void
514:   onPointerUp: () => void
515: }
516: ```
517: 
518: Uses pointer events (not touch events) for broader compatibility.
519: 
520: **Verification**: Unit tests for each composable (Phase 10).
521: 
522: ---
523: 
524: ## Phase 5: Simple Components
525: 
526: ### Step 5.1: Icons (`src/icons/`)
527: 
528: 6 Vue SFC files + `index.ts`. Each icon is a thin wrapper around the same SVG from React.
529: 
530: Example — `SuccessIcon.vue`:
531: ```vue
532: <script setup lang="ts">
533: withDefaults(defineProps<{ size?: number; class?: string }>(), { size: 20 })
534: </script>
535: 
536: <template>
537:   <svg
538:     xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
539:     :width="size"
540:     :height="size"
541:     viewBox="0 0 24 24"
542:     fill="none"
543:     stroke="currentColor"
544:     stroke-width="2"
545:     stroke-linecap="round"
546:     stroke-linejoin="round"
547:     :class="$props.class"
548:   >
549:     <circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10" />
550:     <path d="M9 12l2 2 4-4" />
551:   </svg>
552: </template>
553: ```
554: 
555: Files to create:
556: - `DefaultIcon.vue` — copy SVG paths from React `DefaultIcon.tsx`
557: - `SuccessIcon.vue` — as above
558: - `ErrorIcon.vue` — copy SVG paths from React `ErrorIcon.tsx`
559: - `WarningIcon.vue` — copy SVG paths from React `WarningIcon.tsx`
560: - `InfoIcon.vue` — copy SVG paths from React `InfoIcon.tsx`
561: - `SpinnerIcon.vue` — copy SVG paths from React `SpinnerIcon.tsx`
562: - `index.ts` — re-export all icons
563: 
564: ### Step 5.2: `src/components/AriaLiveAnnouncer.vue`
565: 
566: Port from React `AriaLiveAnnouncer.tsx`. Uses `watch()` on `_announcement` from context.
567: 
568: ```vue
569: <script setup lang="ts">
570: import { ref, watch, nextTick } from 'vue'
571: import { _announcement, type Announcement } from '../context'
572: 
573: const politeMessage = ref('')
574: const assertiveMessage = ref('')
575: 
576: watch(_announcement, (ann: Announcement | null) => {
577:   if (!ann) return
578:   if (ann.politeness === 'assertive') {
579:     assertiveMessage.value = ''
580:     nextTick(() => { assertiveMessage.value = ann.message })
581:   } else {
582:     politeMessage.value = ''
583:     nextTick(() => { politeMessage.value = ann.message })
584:   }
585: })
586: 
587: // Clear after 7 seconds
588: watch(politeMessage, (msg) => {
589:   if (!msg) return
590:   const t = setTimeout(() => { politeMessage.value = '' }, 7000)
591:   // Cleanup not needed for setTimeout in watch — component unmount handles it
592: })
593: watch(assertiveMessage, (msg) => {
594:   if (!msg) return
595:   const t = setTimeout(() => { assertiveMessage.value = '' }, 7000)
596: })
597: </script>
598: 
599: <template>
600:   <div role="status" aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="true"
601:     :style="{ position: 'absolute', width: '1px', height: '1px', padding: 0, margin: '-1px', overflow: 'hidden', clip: 'rect(0,0,0,0)', whiteSpace: 'nowrap', border: 0 }">
602:     {{ politeMessage }}
603:   </div>
604:   <div role="alert" aria-live="assertive" aria-atomic="true"
605:     :style="{ position: 'absolute', width: '1px', height: '1px', padding: 0, margin: '-1px', overflow: 'hidden', clip: 'rect(0,0,0,0)', whiteSpace: 'nowrap', border: 0 }">
606:     {{ assertiveMessage }}
607:   </div>
608: </template>
609: ```
610: 
611: ### Step 5.3: `src/components/ToastErrorBoundary.vue`
612: 
613: Port from React `ToastErrorBoundary.tsx`. Uses `onErrorCaptured()`.
614: 
615: ```vue
616: <script setup lang="ts">
617: import { ref, onErrorCaptured } from 'vue'
618: 
619: const hasError = ref(false)
620: 
621: onErrorCaptured((err) => {
622:   hasError.value = true
623:   if (import.meta.env.DEV) {
624:     console.error('[GooeyToast] Rendering error:', err)
625:   }
626:   return false // prevent propagation
627: })
628: </script>
629: 
630: <template>
631:   <slot v-if="!hasError" />
632: </template>
633: ```
634: 
635: Note: React version renders `null` on error; Vue version renders nothing (empty template).
636: 
637: **Verification**: Component mount tests in Phase 10.
638: 
639: ---
640: 
641: ## Phase 6: ToastContainer.vue
642: 
643: **Goal**: New component that replaces Sonner's `<Toaster>`. Manages toast positioning, stacking, hover expansion.
644: 
645: ### Key responsibilities:
646: 1. `<Teleport to="body">` — render fixed-position `<ol>` outside component tree
647: 2. Render `<li v-for="toast in _toasts">` wrapping `<ToastErrorBoundary><GooeyToast /></ToastErrorBoundary>`
648: 3. Compute stacking offsets: each toast's `translateY` = sum of heights of all newer toasts + gaps
649: 4. Handle hover expand/collapse: `@mouseenter`/`@mouseleave` on `<ol>` sets `containerHovered`
650: 5. Stack scale effect: non-hovered toasts behind front get `scale(1 - i * 0.05)`
651: 
652: ### Props:
653: ```typescript
654: interface ToastContainerProps {
655:   position: ToastPosition
656:   gap: number
657:   offset: number | string
658:   theme: 'light' | 'dark'
659:   dir: 'ltr' | 'rtl'
660: }
661: ```
662: 
663: ### Implementation details:
664: 
665: **Positioning**: CSS `position: fixed`. Position prop maps to CSS:
666: - `bottom-right` → `bottom: {offset}; right: {offset}`
667: - `top-center` → `top: {offset}; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%)`
668: - etc.
669: 
670: **Height tracking**: Each `<GooeyToast>` emits `heightChange(id, height)`. ToastContainer maintains a `Map<id, number>` of current heights. On change, recompute offsets for all toasts.
671: 
672: **Stacking offsets**: `<li>` gets `style.transform = translate3d(0, ${offset}px, 0)`. For bottom positions, offset is negative (toasts stack upward). For top positions, offset is positive.
673: 
674: **Hover behavior**:
675: - `@mouseenter` on `<ol>`: set `containerHovered.value = true`, remove scale/translate compression
676: - `@mouseleave` on `<ol>`: set `containerHovered.value = false`, apply scale/translate compression
677: 
678: **Scale effect** (non-hovered):
679: ```
680: toast[0] (front/newest): scale(1), offset 0
681: toast[1]: scale(0.95), offset = height[0] + gap (compressed)
682: toast[2]: scale(0.90), offset = height[0] + height[1] + 2*gap (compressed)
683: ```
684: When hovered: all toasts scale(1), offset = actual cumulative heights + gaps.
685: 
686: **Transition**: `<li>` gets CSS `transition: transform 0.3s ease, opacity 0.3s ease`.
687: 
688: **Toast removal**: When a toast is dismissed, it's removed from `_toasts` reactively. Vue's `<TransitionGroup>` or manual animation handles the exit.
689: 
690: **Verification**: Stacking offset calculation tests + visual demo.
691: 
692: ---
693: 
694: ## Phase 7: GooeyToast.vue (Core Component)
695: 
696: **Goal**: Port the 1350-line React `GooeyToast.tsx` to a Vue SFC.
697: 
698: This is the most complex step. The component manages:
699: - SVG morph animation (pill → blob → pill)
700: - Spring physics squish effects
701: - State machine (mount → expand → display → pre-dismiss → collapse)
702: - Error shake animation
703: - Hover re-expand
704: - Close button
705: - Progress bar
706: - Swipe-to-dismiss
707: - Action button with success morph-back
708: 
709: ### Architecture decisions:
710: 
711: 1. **Animation controllers** stored as plain variables (not refs) — same as React's `useRef` pattern. Avoids triggering reactivity on animation internals.
712: 
713: 2. **`flush()` function** — direct DOM manipulation for SVG path and content constraints. Same approach as React — avoids reactive overhead on 60fps updates.
714: 
715: 3. **`measure()` function** — reads DOM dimensions. Called via `onMounted`, `ResizeObserver`, and `watch` on props that affect layout.
716: 
717: 4. **State machine** implemented via `watch()` on computed `isExpanded`:
718:    - `isExpanded` true → delay → `showBody = true` → morph 0→1
719:    - `isExpanded` false → morph 1→0 → `showBody = false`
720: 
721: 5. **Timer logic** → `useToastTimer` composable
722: 
723: 6. **Swipe logic** → `useSwipeToDismiss` composable
724: 
725: ### Props (mirrors React `GooeyToastProps`):
726: 
727: ```typescript
728: interface GooeyToastProps {
729:   toast: InternalToast    // full toast data from gooey-toast.ts
730:   toastId: string | number
731: }
732: ```
733: 
734: ### Emits:
735: 
736: ```typescript
737: defineEmits<{
738:   dismiss: [id: string | number]
739:   heightChange: [id: string | number, height: number]
740: }>()
741: ```
742: 
743: ### Key implementation mapping (React → Vue):
744: 
745: | React | Vue |
746: |---|---|
747: | `useState(x)` | `ref(x)` |
748: | `useRef(x)` | `let x = y` (non-reactive) or `ref(x)` if template needs it |
749: | `useEffect(() => { ... }, [deps])` | `watch([deps], () => { ... })` or `watchEffect()` |
750: | `useLayoutEffect` | `onMounted` + `watch({ flush: 'pre' })` |
751: | `useCallback(fn, [deps])` | plain function (Vue doesn't re-create closures) |
752: | `useMemo(fn, [deps])` | `computed(fn)` |
753: | `AnimatePresence` + `motion.div` | `<Transition>` + manual opacity/transform |
754: | `animate(from, to, opts)` | `animate(from, to, opts)` from `motion` package (same API) |
755: | `sonnerToast.dismiss(id)` | `emit('dismiss', id)` |
756: | `subscribeContainerHovered(cb)` | `watch(containerHovered, cb)` |
757: 
758: ### Section-by-section port:
759: 
760: **7a. Template refs & non-reactive state:**
761: - `wrapperRef`, `pathRef`, `headerRef`, `contentRef` → `useTemplateRef<HTMLDivElement>()` or `ref<HTMLElement | null>(null)`
762: - `morphTRef`, `aDims`, `dimsRef`, `expandedDimsRef` → plain `let` variables (non-reactive, only used in imperative animation code)
763: - Animation controllers (`morphCtrl`, `pillResizeCtrl`, etc.) → plain `let` variables
764: 
765: **7b. Reactive state:**
766: - `showBody`, `dismissing`, `hovered`, `actionSuccess`, `progressKey` → `ref()`
767: - `dims` (triggers effects) → `ref({ pw: 0, bw: 0, th: 0 })`
768: 
769: **7c. Computed:**
770: - `effectiveTitle`, `effectivePhase`, `effectiveDescription`, `effectiveAction` → `computed()`
771: - `isExpanded`, `isLoading`, `hasDescription`, `hasAction` → `computed()`
772: - `isRight`, `isCenter` → `computed()` reading from `_config`
773: - `useSpring`, `bounceVal`, `showProgress` → `computed()` merging props + presets + config
774: 
775: **7d. `flush()` and `measure()`:**
776: Direct copy from React. These are plain functions that read refs and write to DOM. No React/Vue dependency.
777: 
778: **7e. Morph animation (Phase 2 in React):**
779: `watch(showBody, (val) => { ... })` — drives morph 0→1 on true, 1→0 on false. Uses `animate()` from `motion` package.
780: 
781: **7f. Pre-dismiss timer:**
782: Delegate to `useToastTimer` composable.
783: 
784: **7g. Re-expand on hover:**
785: `watch([hovered, containerHovered], ...)` — if dismissing and now hovered, stop collapse, re-expand.
786: 
787: **7h. Error shake:**
788: `watch(() => props.toast.phase, (newPhase, oldPhase) => { ... })` — trigger shake animation on error transition.
789: 
790: **7i. Landing squish:**
791: `triggerLandingSquish()` — same imperative function, called from morph watchers.
792: 
793: **7j. Header elastic squish:**
794: `watch([showBody, dismissing, actionSuccess], ...)` — squish header during expand/collapse.
795: 
796: **7k. Icon transition:**
797: Replace `<AnimatePresence mode="wait"><motion.div>` with `<Transition mode="out-in">` using CSS opacity/scale.
798: 
799: **7l. Body content (description, action, timestamp):**
800: Replace `<AnimatePresence>` + `<motion.div>` with `<Transition>` components for fade-in/out.
801: 
802: **7m. Dismiss from container:**
803: Instead of `sonnerToast.dismiss(toastId)`, emit `dismiss` event. ToastContainer handles removal from `_toasts`.
804: 
805: **7n. Height reporting:**
806: On mount and after dimension changes, emit `heightChange` so ToastContainer can update stacking.
807: 
808: **Verification**: Component renders correctly, morph animations work, dismiss lifecycle completes.
809: 
810: ---
811: 
812: ## Phase 8: GooeyToaster.vue
813: 
814: **Goal**: Top-level provider component. Syncs props to `_config`, renders `<ToastContainer>` + `<AriaLiveAnnouncer>`.
815: 
816: ### Props:
817: All `GooeyToasterProps` fields (same as React minus Sonner-specific ones).
818: 
819: ### Implementation:
820: 
821: ```vue
822: <script setup lang="ts">
823: import { watch, onMounted, onUnmounted } from 'vue'
824: import { _config } from '../context'
825: import { gooeyToast, _getMostRecentActiveId } from '../gooey-toast'
826: import { animationPresets } from '../presets'
827: import type { GooeyToasterProps } from '../types'
828: import ToastContainer from './ToastContainer.vue'
829: import AriaLiveAnnouncer from './AriaLiveAnnouncer.vue'
830: 
831: const props = withDefaults(defineProps<GooeyToasterProps>(), {
832:   position: 'bottom-right',
833:   gap: 14,
834:   offset: '24px',
835:   theme: 'light',
836:   visibleToasts: 3,
837:   swipeToDismiss: true,
838:   closeOnEscape: true,
839:   maxQueue: Infinity,
840:   queueOverflow: 'drop-oldest',
841:   showProgress: false,
842:   closeButton: false,
843:   dir: 'ltr',
844: })
845: 
846: // Sync all props to reactive config
847: watch(() => props.position, (v) => { _config.position = v }, { immediate: true })
848: watch(() => props.dir, (v) => { _config.dir = v ?? 'ltr' }, { immediate: true })
849: watch(() => props.theme, (v) => { _config.theme = v }, { immediate: true })
850: // ... etc for all config fields
851: 
852: // Resolve spring/bounce from preset
853: const resolvedSpring = computed(() => props.spring ?? (props.preset ? animationPresets[props.preset]?.spring : undefined) ?? true)
854: const resolvedBounce = computed(() => props.bounce ?? (props.preset ? animationPresets[props.preset]?.bounce : undefined))
855: 
856: watch(resolvedSpring, (v) => { _config.spring = v }, { immediate: true })
857: watch(resolvedBounce, (v) => { _config.bounce = v }, { immediate: true })
858: 
859: // Escape key handler
860: onMounted(() => {
861:   const handler = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
862:     if (!_config.closeOnEscape) return
863:     if (e.key === 'Escape') {
864:       const id = _getMostRecentActiveId()
865:       if (id != null) gooeyToast.dismiss(id)
866:     }
867:   }
868:   document.addEventListener('keydown', handler)
869:   onUnmounted(() => document.removeEventListener('keydown', handler))
870: })
871: 
872: // CSS detection warning (dev only)
873: onMounted(() => {
874:   if (import.meta.env.DEV) {
875:     const el = document.createElement('div')
876:     el.setAttribute('data-gooey-toast-css', '')
877:     el.style.cssText = 'position:absolute;width:0;height:0;overflow:hidden;pointer-events:none'
878:     document.body.appendChild(el)
879:     const value = getComputedStyle(el).getPropertyValue('--gooey-toast')
880:     document.body.removeChild(el)
881:     if (!value) {
882:       console.warn('[gooey-toast] Styles not found. Import CSS:\n\n  import "goey-toast-vue/styles.css";\n')
883:     }
884:   }
885: })
886: </script>
887: 
888: <template>
889:   <ToastContainer
890:     :position="props.position"
891:     :gap="props.gap"
892:     :offset="props.offset"
893:     :theme="props.theme"
894:     :dir="props.dir ?? 'ltr'"
895:   />
896:   <AriaLiveAnnouncer />
897: </template>
898: ```
899: 
900: **Verification**: Props sync to `_config`, Escape key dismisses toasts, CSS detection works.
901: 
902: ---
903: 
904: ## Phase 9: Public API + CSS
905: 
906: ### Step 9.1: `src/index.ts`
907: 
908: ```typescript
909: import './components/GooeyToast.css'
910: 
911: export { default as GooeyToaster } from './components/GooeyToaster.vue'
912: export { gooeyToast } from './gooey-toast'
913: export { animationPresets } from './presets'
914: export type { AnimationPreset, AnimationPresetName } from './presets'
915: export type {
916:   GooeyToastOptions,
917:   GooeyPromiseData,
918:   GooeyToasterProps,
919:   GooeyToastAction,
920:   GooeyToastClassNames,
921:   GooeyToastTimings,
922:   GooeyToastUpdateOptions,
923:   DismissFilter,
924:   ToastPosition,
925: } from './types'
926: 
927: // Backward-compatible aliases
928: export { default as GoeyToaster } from './components/GooeyToaster.vue'
929: export { gooeyToast as goeyToast } from './gooey-toast'
930: ```
931: 
932: ### Step 9.2: `src/components/GooeyToast.css`
933: 
934: Copy from React `GooeyToast.css`. Changes:
935: - **Remove** all Sonner-specific selectors:
936:   - `[data-sonner-toast]` rules (lines 12-18, 37-39)
937:   - `[data-sonner-toaster]` rules (lines 21-27)
938:   - `[data-x-position="center"]` rules
939:   - `[data-expanded="true"]` transition override
940: - **Add** ToastContainer positioning styles:
941: 
942: ```css
943: /* Toast container — fixed position overlay */
944: .gooey-toast-container {
945:   position: fixed;
946:   z-index: 999999;
947:   pointer-events: none;
948:   list-style: none;
949:   margin: 0;
950:   padding: 0;
951: }
952: 
953: .gooey-toast-container > li {
954:   pointer-events: auto;
955:   transition: transform 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1),
956:               opacity 0.3s ease;
957: }
958: ```
959: 
960: - **Keep** everything else unchanged (wrapper, content, header, title colors, description, action buttons, progress bar, dark mode, close button, spinner)
961: 
962: **Verification**: CSS loads with `--gooey-toast: 1` detection marker intact.
963: 
964: ---
965: 
966: ## Phase 10: Tests
967: 
968: ### Test file mapping (React → Vue):
969: 
970: | React test | Vue test | Notes |
971: |---|---|---|
972: | `gooey-toast.test.tsx` | `gooey-toast.test.ts` | No JSX — tests pure functions + reactive state |
973: | `GooeyToast.test.tsx` | `GooeyToast.test.ts` | Uses `@vue/test-utils` mount |
974: | `GooeyToaster.test.tsx` | `GooeyToaster.test.ts` | Uses `@vue/test-utils` mount |
975: | `AriaLiveAnnouncer.test.tsx` | `AriaLiveAnnouncer.test.ts` | Watch-based |
976: | `ToastErrorBoundary.test.tsx` | `ToastErrorBoundary.test.ts` | onErrorCaptured |
977: | `usePrefersReducedMotion.test.tsx` | `usePrefersReducedMotion.test.ts` | Composable test |
978: | — | `morph.test.ts` | **New** — pure function tests |
979: | — | `spring.test.ts` | **New** — pure function tests |
980: | — | `ToastContainer.test.ts` | **New** — stacking, positioning |
981: 
982: ### Test approach:
983: 
984: **Pure function tests** (`morph.test.ts`, `spring.test.ts`):
985: - Test morph path at t=0 (pill), t=1 (full blob), intermediate values
986: - Test center morph path symmetry
987: - Test memoizePath caching
988: - Test squishSpring parameter ranges
989: - Snapshot SVG path strings for regression
990: 
991: **State management tests** (`gooey-toast.test.ts`):
992: - `createGooeyToast()` adds to `_toasts`
993: - Queue: 4th toast goes to queue when `visibleToasts=3`
994: - `dismissGooeyToast(id)` removes by ID
995: - `dismissGooeyToast({ type: 'error' })` removes by type filter
996: - `dismissGooeyToast()` clears all
997: - `updateGooeyToast()` mutates in-place
998: - Promise toast: loading → success transition
999: - Promise toast: loading → error transition
1000: - Callbacks: `onDismiss` called on dismiss
1001: - Callbacks: `onAutoClose` called on auto-dismiss, NOT on manual dismiss
1002: 
1003: **Component tests** (using `@vue/test-utils`):
1004: - Mock `motion`'s `animate()` to return `{ stop: () => {} }`
1005: - Mount with minimal props, verify renders
1006: - Verify ARIA roles (status vs alert based on type)
1007: - Verify description renders when provided
1008: - Verify action button triggers callback
1009: 
1010: **Verification**: `npm run test` passes all tests.
1011:

1047: 
1048: ---
1049: 
1050: ## Critical Implementation Notes
1051: 
1052: ### What can be directly copied (zero changes):
1053: - `presets.ts`
1054: - `gooey-styles.ts`
1055: - SVG morph math (`morphPathRaw`, `morphPathCenterRaw`)
1056: - Spring physics (`squishSpring`)
1057: - `SMOOTH_EASE`, `PH`, `DEFAULT_DISPLAY_DURATION`
1058: - Icon SVG paths
1059: - All CSS class names and most CSS rules
1060: - `buildAnnouncementMessage()`, `getAnnouncePoliteness()`
1061: 
1062: ### What requires architectural rewrite:
1063: - `gooey-toast.ts` — completely different lifecycle (no Sonner, no React components)
1064: - `ToastContainer.vue` — entirely new (replaces Sonner)
1065: - `GooeyToast.vue` — same logic, different framework primitives
1066: - `GooeyToaster.vue` — much simpler (no Sonner `<Toaster>`, no MutationObserver hacks)
1067: - `context.ts` — reactive() replaces getter/setter/pub-sub pattern
1068: 
1069: ### What is eliminated entirely:
1070: - All `syncSonnerHeights()` code — not needed without Sonner
1071: - `registerSonnerObserver()` — not needed without Sonner
1072: - `GooeyToastWrapper` / `PromiseToastWrapper` React components — replaced by reactive data
1073: - React StrictMode double-mount guards
1074: - Sonner's `toast.custom()` / `toast.dismiss()` calls
1075: - `toastOptions`, `richColors`, `expand` props (Sonner-specific)
1076: 
1077: ### Risk areas:
1078: 1. **Animation timing parity** — Motion's `animate()` API should be identical to Framer Motion's standalone version, but verify spring parameter interpretation matches.
1079: 2. **ToastContainer stacking** — The most novel code (replaces Sonner). Needs careful height tracking and offset calculation.
1080: 3. **GooeyToast.vue complexity** — 1350 lines of animation logic. Port section by section, verify each animation works before moving to the next.
1081: 4. **Icon cross-fade** — `<Transition mode="out-in">` may not perfectly match `<AnimatePresence mode="wait">` timing. May need manual transition hooks.

(End of file - total 1081 lines)
</content>
```
