## Assistant (Compaction · Claude Opus 4.6 · 128.9s)



## Goal

Port goey-toast-react library to goey-toast-vue (Vue 3) with 100% feature parity, consistent public API, and Vue-idiomatic internal implementation. The project follows an 11-phase implementation plan.

## Instructions

- Read and follow the implementation plan at `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-goey-toast-vue.md` — it is the authoritative execution guide with 11 phases
- The design spec at `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-17-goey-toast-vue-design.md` contains all approved architectural decisions
- User chose: Motion (motion.dev) as animation engine, from-scratch toast container (no Sonner), Vue 3 only (>=3.3), 100% feature parity, API aligned with React version, Vue idiomatic rewrite (方案 A)
- About 40% of code can be directly copied (pure functions, CSS, class name maps); 60% needs rewrite
- Key React→Vue mappings: `useState` → `ref()`, `useEffect` → `watch()`, `useCallback` → plain function, `useMemo` → `computed()`, `AnimatePresence` → `<Transition>`, Sonner's `toast.custom()/toast.dismiss()` → reactive `_toasts` array, getter/setter + pub/sub → `reactive()` singleton
- Build system: Vite lib mode + @vitejs/plugin-vue + vite-plugin-dts (not tsup, because SFC needs vue plugin)
- Comment policy: The project has a hook that flags all comments/docstrings. Only necessary comments allowed (mathematical formulas, intentionally empty catch blocks, TypeScript triple-slash directives). Remove all unnecessary comments.

## Discoveries

- React version heavily hacks Sonner (syncSonnerHeights, registerSonnerObserver, MutationObserver tricks) — ALL eliminated in Vue port since we build our own ToastContainer
- React StrictMode double-mount guards (mountedRef + setTimeout pattern) not needed in Vue
- GooeyToastWrapper and PromiseToastWrapper React components are eliminated — ToastContainer renders directly from reactive toast data
- Motion's `animate()` API is nearly identical to Framer Motion's standalone version, making animation code portable
- The core GooeyToast.tsx is 1350 lines with complex animation state machine: mount → expand delay (330ms) → showBody=true → morphT 0→1 → display timer → pre-dismiss → morphT 1→0 → emit dismiss
- SVG morph path generation (morphPathRaw, morphPathCenterRaw) and spring physics (squishSpring) are pure functions — direct copy with zero changes
- `DEFAULT_EXPANDED_DURATION` constant was removed from gooey-toast.ts because in the Vue architecture, timer defaults are handled by the `useToastTimer` composable rather than at the toast creation level
- The swipe-to-dismiss implementation uses touch events (matching React source) rather than pointer events (which the plan originally suggested)
- ToastContainer.vue is a novel component replacing Sonner's stacking — uses fixed positioning, absolute-positioned `<li>` with translate3d, scale compression when not hovered, expandedHeight-based `<ol>` sizing for hover detection
- A stub GooeyToast.vue was created in Phase 6 (just props/emits/minimal template) so ToastContainer can import it and typecheck passes. This stub must be replaced with the full implementation in Phase 7.
- For GooeyToast.vue icon rendering: need a `RenderVNode` helper component (using `defineComponent` with a render function) to handle VNode | string content in SFC templates
- `SMOOTH_EASE` readonly tuple needs `[...SMOOTH_EASE]` spread or cast when passed to motion's `animate()` ease parameter for TypeScript compatibility
- Height reporting to ToastContainer: use ResizeObserver on wrapper element, emit `heightChange` events
- Watcher cleanup in Vue 3: use the 3rd argument `onCleanup` in watch callbacks (e.g., `watch(source, (val, old, onCleanup) => { onCleanup(() => clearTimeout(t)) })`)
- For `useLayoutEffect` equivalents in Vue: use `{ flush: 'post' }` on watch for measure and dims watchers (runs after DOM update but before browser paint, matching React's useLayoutEffect behavior)
- **BUG FOUND in useToastTimer.ts**: The `resumeTimer()` function incorrectly bails when `remaining === null`. When a toast is re-expanded (user hovers during collapse), the composable resets `remaining = null` and calls `startTimer()` which bails because paused. Then on unhover, `resumeTimer()` checks `remaining === null` and also bails — timer never restarts. **FIX**: Change `if (remaining === null || remaining <= 0) return` to `if (remaining !== null && remaining <= 0) return` so `remaining === null` means "start fresh with full delay"
- Risk areas: (1) Motion animate() spring parameter interpretation parity, (2) ToastContainer stacking is novel code replacing Sonner, (3) GooeyToast.vue is 1350 lines needing section-by-section port

## Accomplished

**Completed (across previous sessions):**
- ✅ Brainstorming with user — all clarifying questions answered, all design choices confirmed
- ✅ Read ALL React source files in full detail
- ✅ Design spec written and committed: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-17-goey-toast-vue-design.md` (commit d90afe2)

**Completed (implementation):**
- ✅ **Phase 1: Scaffolding** — package.json, tsconfig.json, vite.config.ts, vitest.config.ts, env.d.ts, src/index.ts (placeholder), src/__tests__/setup.ts. `npm install` and `npm run build` verified working.
- ✅ **Phase 2: Pure functions & types** — All 5 files written and typecheck clean:
  - `src/types.ts` — All types ported (ReactNode → VNode | string, Sonner types removed, ToastPosition inline union added)
  - `src/presets.ts` — Direct copy from React
  - `src/morph.ts` — Extracted morph functions from GooeyToast.tsx (morphPathRaw, morphPathCenterRaw, memoizePath, PH constant)
  - `src/spring.ts` — Extracted spring physics (squishSpring, DEFAULT_EXPAND_DUR, DEFAULT_COLLAPSE_DUR, SMOOTH_EASE)
  - `src/components/gooey-styles.ts` — Direct copy of class name map
- ✅ **Phase 3: State management** — Both files written and typecheck clean:
  - `src/context.ts` — Reactive singleton replacing getter/setter/pub-sub pattern (_config reactive, containerHovered ref, _announcement ref, announce function)
  - `src/gooey-toast.ts` — Complete rewrite: reactive _toasts array replaces Sonner, InternalToast interface, queue logic, dismiss logic (by id/filter/all), promise toast handling with .then/.catch mutating reactive data, ARIA announcements, public gooeyToast API
- ✅ **Phase 4: Composables** — All 3 files written and typecheck verified clean:
  - `src/composables/usePrefersReducedMotion.ts` — matchMedia listener, readonly ref
  - `src/composables/useToastTimer.ts` — Timer with pause/resume/restart, progress duration tracking (**has a bug in resumeTimer — needs fix, see Discoveries**)
  - `src/composables/useSwipeToDismiss.ts` — Touch-based swipe gesture with threshold, opacity feedback
- ✅ **Phase 5: Simple components** — All 9 files written and typecheck verified clean:
  - `src/icons/DefaultIcon.vue`, `SuccessIcon.vue`, `ErrorIcon.vue`, `WarningIcon.vue`, `InfoIcon.vue`, `SpinnerIcon.vue` — 6 SVG icon SFCs with `size` + `class` props
  - `src/icons/index.ts` — re-exports all icons
  - `src/components/AriaLiveAnnouncer.vue` — watches `_announcement` ref, polite/assertive dual live regions, 7s auto-clear with proper watch cleanup
  - `src/components/ToastErrorBoundary.vue` — `onErrorCaptured` + slot rendering
- ✅ **Phase 6: ToastContainer.vue** — Written and typecheck verified clean:
  - `src/components/ToastContainer.vue` — Replaces Sonner's stacking: Teleport to body, fixed-position `<ol>`, computed stacking offsets with translate3d + scale, hover expansion via containerHovered, height tracking via Map, visibility control based on visibleToasts
  - A **stub** `src/components/GooeyToast.vue` was created (just props/emits/minimal `<div>{{ toast.title }}</div>`) so ToastContainer imports compile

**In Progress:**
- 🔄 **Phase 7: GooeyToast.vue** — The full React source (all 1350 lines) has been re-read this session. The complete React→Vue mapping has been deeply analyzed and a detailed architecture for the Vue SFC has been planned (see Active Working Context below). The stub file exists and needs to be replaced with the full implementation. A bug in useToastTimer.ts was discovered during the analysis and needs to be fixed first.

**Not started — remaining phases:**
- Phase 8: GooeyToaster.vue (top-level provider)
- Phase 9: Public API (index.ts) + CSS adaptation
- Phase 10: Tests
- Phase 11: Build & verify

## 5. Active Working Context (For Seamless Continuation)

**Files**: The next file to write is `goey-toast-vue/src/components/GooeyToast.vue` (replace stub). First fix `goey-toast-vue/src/composables/useToastTimer.ts` (resumeTimer bug). Reference: `goey-toast-react/src/components/GooeyToast.tsx` (1350 lines, fully read).

**Code in Progress — GooeyToast.vue complete architecture** (planned in detail but not yet written):

- **Imports**: `ref, computed, watch, onMounted, onUnmounted, defineComponent, h` from vue; `animate` from motion; all composables, context, morph, spring, icons, styles, types
- **Props/Emits**: `{ toast: InternalToast, toastId: string | number }`, emits `dismiss` and `heightChange`
- **RenderVNode helper**: `defineComponent({ props: { content: null }, setup(p) { return () => p.content } })` — renders VNode | string in SFC templates
- **Phase/color maps**: `phaseIconMap`, `titleColorMap`, `actionColorMap`, `progressColorMap` — same as React
- **Config-derived computeds**: `prefersReducedMotion` (composable), `presetConfig`, `useSpringVal`, `bounceVal`, `showProgressBar`, `showCloseButton`, `fillColor`, `isCenter`, `posIsRight`, `isRight`
- **Reactive state** (refs): `actionSuccess<string|null>`, `dismissing<boolean>`, `progressKey<number>`, `hovered<boolean>`, `showBody<boolean>`, `dims<{pw,bw,th}>`
- **Non-reactive state** (plain `let`): `morphT`, `aDims`, `expandedDims`, `collapsing`, `preDismiss`, `collapseEndTime`, `reExpanding`, `lastSquishTime`, `prevPhaseVal`, `headerSquishedVal`, `mountSquishedVal`, `prevShowBodyVal`
- **Animation controllers** (plain `let`): `morphCtrl`, `pillResizeCtrl`, `headerSquishCtrl`, `blobSquishCtrl`, `shakeCtrl` — all typed as `{ stop: () => void } | null`
- **Template refs**: `wrapperRef`, `pathRef`, `headerRef`, `contentRef`
- **Effective values** (computed): `effectiveTitle`, `effectivePhase`, `effectiveDescription`, `effectiveAction`, `isLoading`, `hasDescription`, `hasAction`, `isExpanded`, `hasDims`
- **Composables**: `useToastTimer` with `timerPaused = computed(() => hovered || containerHovered)`, `timerEnabled = computed(() => showBody && !actionSuccess && !dismissing)`, `onTimeout` saves expandedDims + sets collapsing/preDismiss flags + calls `_markAutoClose(toastId)` + sets dismissing=true. `useSwipeToDismiss` with `onDismiss: () => emit('dismiss', toastId)`
- **flush()**: Direct DOM manipulation for SVG path + content constraints — direct port from React lines 462-545. Reads `isCenter.value` and `isRight.value` instead of `getGooeyPosition()/getGooeyDir()`. Uses `dims.value.bw` instead of `dimsRef.current.bw`.
- **measure()**: Read DOM dims — port from React lines 548-573. Temporarily clears constraints, measures, restores. Updates both `dimsVal` (non-reactive) and `dims.value` (reactive).
- **emitHeight()**: ResizeObserver on wrapperRef + explicit calls after key animation milestones. Deduplicates via `lastEmittedHeight`.
- **triggerLandingSquish()**: Spring animation — port from React lines 599-633. Checks `prefersReducedMotion`, `useSpringVal`, 300ms cooldown. Uses `blobSquishCtrl`.
- **handleActionClick()**: Saves expandedDims, sets collapsing, sets actionSuccess. Calls onClick in try/catch.
- **Computed styles**: `basePositionStyle` (margin/transform for position), `wrapperStyle` (merges base + swipe), `contentStyle` (textAlign + scaleX flip), `closeButtonStyle`, `ariaRole`, `ariaLive`

**Watchers (in execution order):**
1. Measure on prop changes: `watch([effectiveTitle, effectivePhase, isExpanded, showBody, effectiveDescription, effectiveAction], measure + setTimeout(measure,100), { flush: 'post' })`
2. ResizeObserver on contentRef: `onMounted()` setup
3. Dims → pill resize animation: `watch(dims, ..., { flush: 'post' })` — animate pill resize in compact mode, immediate update during morph or expanded
4. Mount squish: `watch(hasDims, ...)` — trigger landing squish after 45ms delay for simple toasts
5. ShowBody expand squish: `watch(showBody, ...)` — trigger expand squish after 80ms (skip if hovered)
6. isExpanded → expand delay/collapse: `watch(isExpanded, ...)` — true: 330ms delay → showBody=true; false: animate morph 1→0, onComplete → showBody=false
7. showBody → morph pill↔blob: `watch(showBody, ...)` — true: rAF → animate morph 0→1; false: reset morphT=0, flush
8. Error shake: `watch(() => props.toast.phase, ...)` — shake animation on error transition
9. Header elastic squish: `watch([showBody, dismissing, actionSuccess], ...)` — squish down on expand, spring back on collapse
10. Re-expand on hover: `watch([hovered, containerHovered, dismissing], ...)` — stop collapse, reset states, drive expand morph from current position via rAF
11. Dismiss after collapse: `watch([dismissing, showBody], ...)` — 800ms delay → emit('dismiss', toastId), guard with hovered/containerHovered refs
12. Dismiss after action success: `watch([actionSuccess, showBody], ...)` — 1200ms delay → emit('dismiss', toastId)

**Template structure:**
- Wrapper div with mouse/touch events, aria attrs, data-center, data-theme
- SVG background with path ref (overflow visible, absolute positioned)
- Close button (conditional on showCloseButton && !loading)
- Content div with header (icon + title + optional inline timestamp), description (Transition), timestamp-body (Transition), action (Transition with delay), progress bar
- Icon uses `<Transition mode="out-in" :name="prefersReducedMotion ? '' : 'gooey-icon'">` with keyed div wrapping `<RenderVNode>`
- Body transitions use `<Transition :name="prefersReducedMotion ? '' : 'gooey-fade'">`
- Scoped CSS for transition classes (gooey-icon-enter-active/leave-active, gooey-fade-enter-active/leave-active)

**Key eliminations from React**: Remove ALL `syncSonnerHeights` (~70 lines), `registerSonnerObserver` (~35 lines), MutationObserver code (~25 lines), `observerRegistry` (~10 lines). Replace `sonnerToast.dismiss(id)` with `emit('dismiss', id)`. Replace `subscribeContainerHovered` with direct `watch(containerHovered)`. Remove useIsomorphicLayoutEffect wrapper.

**External References**:
- Motion (motion.dev) `animate()` API — `animate(from, to, { type: 'spring', stiffness, damping, mass, onUpdate, onComplete })` and `animate(from, to, { duration, ease, onUpdate, onComplete })`
- Vue 3 Composition API, `<Transition>`, `<Teleport>`, watch with onCleanup (3rd argument)
- Plan Phase 7 section: lines 694-808 of `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-17-goey-toast-vue.md`

**State & Variables**: Git repo has 2 commits on main (spec + plan); all implementation code is uncommitted; `npm install` done; `npm run build` verified working with placeholder index.ts; `npx vue-tsc --noEmit` passes clean with all current files

Find [X] patterns in the codebase - show file paths, implementation approach, and conventions used. I'll use this to [HOW RESULTS WILL BE USED]. task(subagent_type="oracle", load_skills=[], prompt="I need architectural review of my approach to [TASK]. Here's my plan: [DESCRIBE PLAN WITH SPECIFIC FILES AND CHANGES]. My concerns are: [LIST SPECIFIC UNCERTAINTIES]. Please evaluate: correctness of approach, potential issues I'm missing, and whether a better alternative exists.", run_in_background=false)

## MANDATORY: PLAN AGENT INVOCATION (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

**YOU MUST ALWAYS INVOKE THE PLAN AGENT FOR ANY NON-TRIVIAL TASK.**

| Condition | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Task has 2+ steps | MUST call plan agent |
| Task scope unclear | MUST call plan agent |
| Implementation required | MUST call plan agent |
| Architecture decision needed | MUST call plan agent |

```
task(subagent_type="plan", load_skills=[], run_in_background=false, prompt="<gathered context + user request>")
```

**WHY PLAN AGENT IS MANDATORY:**
- Plan agent analyzes dependencies and parallel execution opportunities
- Plan agent outputs a **parallel task graph** with waves and dependencies
- Plan agent provides structured TODO list with category + skills per task
- YOU are an orchestrator, NOT an implementer

### SESSION CONTINUITY WITH PLAN AGENT (CRITICAL)

**Plan agent returns a session_id. USE IT for follow-up interactions.**

| Scenario | Action |
|----------|--------|
| Plan agent asks clarifying questions | `task(session_id="{returned_session_id}", load_skills=[], run_in_background=false, prompt="<your answer>")` |
| Need to refine the plan | `task(session_id="{returned_session_id}", load_skills=[], run_in_background=false, prompt="Please adjust: <feedback>")` |
| Plan needs more detail | `task(session_id="{returned_session_id}", load_skills=[], run_in_background=false, prompt="Add more detail to Task N")` |

**WHY SESSION_ID IS CRITICAL:**
- Plan agent retains FULL conversation context
- No repeated exploration or context gathering
- Saves 70%+ tokens on follow-ups
- Maintains interview continuity until plan is finalized

```
// WRONG: Starting fresh loses all context
task(subagent_type="plan", load_skills=[], run_in_background=false, prompt="Here's more info...")

// CORRECT: Resume preserves everything
task(session_id="ses_abc123", load_skills=[], run_in_background=false, prompt="Here's my answer to your question: ...")
```

**FAILURE TO CALL PLAN AGENT = INCOMPLETE WORK.**

---

## AGENTS / **CATEGORY + SKILLS** UTILIZATION PRINCIPLES

**DEFAULT BEHAVIOR: DELEGATE. DO NOT WORK YOURSELF.**

| Task Type | Action | Why |
|-----------|--------|-----|
| Codebase exploration | task(subagent_type="explore", load_skills=[], run_in_background=true) | Parallel, context-efficient |
| Documentation lookup | task(subagent_type="librarian", load_skills=[], run_in_background=true) | Specialized knowledge |
| Planning | task(subagent_type="plan", load_skills=[], run_in_background=false) | Parallel task graph + structured TODO list |
| Hard problem (conventional) | task(subagent_type="oracle", load_skills=[], run_in_background=false) | Architecture, debugging, complex logic |
| Hard problem (non-conventional) | task(category="artistry", load_skills=[...], run_in_background=true) | Different approach needed |
| Implementation | task(category="...", load_skills=[...], run_in_background=true) | Domain-optimized models |

**CATEGORY + SKILL DELEGATION:**
```
// Frontend work
task(category="visual-engineering", load_skills=["frontend-ui-ux"], run_in_background=true)

// Complex logic
task(category="ultrabrain", load_skills=["typescript-programmer"], run_in_background=true)

// Quick fixes
task(category="quick", load_skills=["git-master"], run_in_background=true)
```

**YOU SHOULD ONLY DO IT YOURSELF WHEN:**
- Task is trivially simple (1-2 lines, obvious change)
- You have ALL context already loaded
- Delegation overhead exceeds task complexity

**OTHERWISE: DELEGATE. ALWAYS.**

---

## EXECUTION RULES
- **TODO**: Track EVERY step. Mark complete IMMEDIATELY after each.
- **PARALLEL**: Fire independent agent calls simultaneously via task(run_in_background=true) - NEVER wait sequentially.
- **BACKGROUND FIRST**: Use task for exploration/research agents (10+ concurrent if needed).
- **VERIFY**: Re-read request after completion. Check ALL requirements met before reporting done.
- **DELEGATE**: Don't do everything yourself - orchestrate specialized agents for their strengths.

## WORKFLOW
1. Analyze the request and identify required capabilities
2. Spawn exploration/librarian agents via task(run_in_background=true) in PARALLEL (10+ if needed)
3. Use Plan agent with gathered context to create detailed work breakdown
4. Execute with continuous verification against original requirements

## VERIFICATION GUARANTEE (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

**NOTHING is "done" without PROOF it works.**

### Pre-Implementation: Define Success Criteria

BEFORE writing ANY code, you MUST define:

| Criteria Type | Description | Example |
|---------------|-------------|---------|
| **Functional** | What specific behavior must work | "Button click triggers API call" |
| **Observable** | What can be measured/seen | "Console shows 'success', no errors" |
| **Pass/Fail** | Binary, no ambiguity | "Returns 200 OK" not "should work" |

Write these criteria explicitly. **Record them in your TODO/Task items.** Each task MUST include a "QA: [how to verify]" field. These criteria are your CONTRACT - work toward them, verify against them.

### Test Plan Template (MANDATORY for non-trivial tasks)

```
## Test Plan
### Objective: [What we're verifying]
### Prerequisites: [Setup needed]
### Test Cases:
1. [Test Name]: [Input] → [Expected Output] → [How to verify]
2. ...
### Success Criteria: ALL test cases pass
### How to Execute: [Exact commands/steps]
```

### Execution & Evidence Requirements

| Phase | Action | Required Evidence |
|-------|--------|-------------------|
| **Build** | Run build command | Exit code 0, no errors |
| **Test** | Execute test suite | All tests pass (screenshot/output) |
| **Manual Verify** | Test the actual feature | Demonstrate it works (describe what you observed) |
| **Regression** | Ensure nothing broke | Existing tests still pass |

**WITHOUT evidence = NOT verified = NOT done.**

<MANUAL_QA_MANDATE>
### YOU MUST EXECUTE MANUAL QA YOURSELF. THIS IS NOT OPTIONAL.

**YOUR FAILURE MODE**: You finish coding, run lsp_diagnostics, and declare "done" without actually TESTING the feature. lsp_diagnostics catches type errors, NOT functional bugs. Your work is NOT verified until you MANUALLY test it.

**WHAT MANUAL QA MEANS - execute ALL that apply:**

| If your change... | YOU MUST... |
|---|---|
| Adds/modifies a CLI command | Run the command with Bash. Show the output. |
| Changes build output | Run the build. Verify the output files exist and are correct. |
| Modifies API behavior | Call the endpoint. Show the response. |
| Changes UI rendering | Describe what renders. Use a browser tool if available. |
| Adds a new tool/hook/feature | Test it end-to-end in a real scenario. |
| Modifies config handling | Load the config. Verify it parses correctly. |

**UNACCEPTABLE QA CLAIMS:**
- "This should work" - RUN IT.
- "The types check out" - Types don't catch logic bugs. RUN IT.
- "lsp_diagnostics is clean" - That's a TYPE check, not a FUNCTIONAL check. RUN IT.
- "Tests pass" - Tests cover known cases. Does the ACTUAL FEATURE work as the user expects? RUN IT.

**You have Bash, you have tools. There is ZERO excuse for not running manual QA.**
**Manual QA is the FINAL gate before reporting completion. Skip it and your work is INCOMPLETE.**
</MANUAL_QA_MANDATE>

### TDD Workflow (when test infrastructure exists)

1. **SPEC**: Define what "working" means (success criteria above)
2. **RED**: Write failing test → Run it → Confirm it FAILS
3. **GREEN**: Write minimal code → Run test → Confirm it PASSES
4. **REFACTOR**: Clean up → Tests MUST stay green
5. **VERIFY**: Run full test suite, confirm no regressions
6. **EVIDENCE**: Report what you ran and what output you saw

### Verification Anti-Patterns (BLOCKING)

| Violation | Why It Fails |
|-----------|--------------|
| "It should work now" | No evidence. Run it. |
| "I added the tests" | Did they pass? Show output. |
| "Fixed the bug" | How do you know? What did you test? |
| "Implementation complete" | Did you verify against success criteria? |
| Skipping test execution | Tests exist to be RUN, not just written |

**CLAIM NOTHING WITHOUT PROOF. EXECUTE. VERIFY. SHOW EVIDENCE.**

## ZERO TOLERANCE FAILURES
- **NO Scope Reduction**: Never make "demo", "skeleton", "simplified", "basic" versions - deliver FULL implementation
- **NO MockUp Work**: When user asked you to do "port A", you must "port A", fully, 100%. No Extra feature, No reduced feature, no mock data, fully working 100% port.
- **NO Partial Completion**: Never stop at 60-80% saying "you can extend this..." - finish 100%
- **NO Assumed Shortcuts**: Never skip requirements you deem "optional" or "can be added later"
- **NO Premature Stopping**: Never declare done until ALL TODOs are completed and verified
- **NO TEST DELETION**: Never delete or skip failing tests to make the build pass. Fix the code, not the tests.

THE USER ASKED FOR X. DELIVER EXACTLY X. NOT A SUBSET. NOT A DEMO. NOT A STARTING POINT.

1. EXPLORES + LIBRARIANS
2. GATHER -> PLAN AGENT SPAWN
3. WORK BY DELEGATING TO ANOTHER AGENTS

NOW.

</ultrawork-mode>

## Command Instructions

<command-instruction>
You are starting an ULTRAWORK Loop - a self-referential development loop that runs until verified completion.

## How ULTRAWORK Loop Works

1. You will work on the task continuously
2. When you believe the work is complete, output: `<promise>{{COMPLETION_PROMISE}}</promise>`
3. That does NOT finish the loop yet. The system will require Oracle verification
4. The loop only ends after the system confirms Oracle verified the result
5. The iteration limit is 500 for ultrawork mode, 100 for normal mode

## Rules

- Focus on finishing the task completely
- After you emit the completion promise, run Oracle verification when instructed
- Do not treat DONE as final completion until Oracle verifies it

## Exit Conditions

1. **Verified Completion**: Oracle verifies the result and the system confirms it
2. **Cancel**: User runs `/cancel-ralph`

## Your Task

Parse the arguments below and begin working on the task. The format is:
`"task description" [--completion-promise=TEXT] [--strategy=reset|continue]`

Default completion promise is "DONE".
</command-instruction>

<user-task>
完成 Phase 7 后停止。
</user-task>

<type>file</type>
<content>
694: ## Phase 7: GooeyToast.vue (Core Component)
695: 
696: **Goal**: Port the 1350-line React `GooeyToast.tsx` to a Vue SFC.
697: 
698: This is the most complex step. The component manages:
699: - SVG morph animation (pill → blob → pill)
700: - Spring physics squish effects
701: - State machine (mount → expand → display → pre-dismiss → collapse)
702: - Error shake animation
703: - Hover re-expand
704: - Close button
705: - Progress bar
706: - Swipe-to-dismiss
707: - Action button with success morph-back
708: 
709: ### Architecture decisions:
710: 
711: 1. **Animation controllers** stored as plain variables (not refs) — same as React's `useRef` pattern. Avoids triggering reactivity on animation internals.
712: 
713: 2. **`flush()` function** — direct DOM manipulation for SVG path and content constraints. Same approach as React — avoids reactive overhead on 60fps updates.
714: 
715: 3. **`measure()` function** — reads DOM dimensions. Called via `onMounted`, `ResizeObserver`, and `watch` on props that affect layout.
716: 
717: 4. **State machine** implemented via `watch()` on computed `isExpanded`:
718:    - `isExpanded` true → delay → `showBody = true` → morph 0→1
719:    - `isExpanded` false → morph 1→0 → `showBody = false`
720: 
721: 5. **Timer logic** → `useToastTimer` composable
722: 
723: 6. **Swipe logic** → `useSwipeToDismiss` composable
724: 
725: ### Props (mirrors React `GooeyToastProps`):
726: 
727: ```typescript
728: interface GooeyToastProps {
729:   toast: InternalToast    // full toast data from gooey-toast.ts
730:   toastId: string | number
731: }
732: ```
733: 
734: ### Emits:
735: 
736: ```typescript
737: defineEmits<{
738:   dismiss: [id: string | number]
739:   heightChange: [id: string | number, height: number]
740: }>()
741: ```
742: 
743: ### Key implementation mapping (React → Vue):
744: 
745: | React | Vue |
746: |---|---|
747: | `useState(x)` | `ref(x)` |
748: | `useRef(x)` | `let x = y` (non-reactive) or `ref(x)` if template needs it |
749: | `useEffect(() => { ... }, [deps])` | `watch([deps], () => { ... })` or `watchEffect()` |
750: | `useLayoutEffect` | `onMounted` + `watch({ flush: 'pre' })` |
751: | `useCallback(fn, [deps])` | plain function (Vue doesn't re-create closures) |
752: | `useMemo(fn, [deps])` | `computed(fn)` |
753: | `AnimatePresence` + `motion.div` | `<Transition>` + manual opacity/transform |
754: | `animate(from, to, opts)` | `animate(from, to, opts)` from `motion` package (same API) |
755: | `sonnerToast.dismiss(id)` | `emit('dismiss', id)` |
756: | `subscribeContainerHovered(cb)` | `watch(containerHovered, cb)` |
757: 
758: ### Section-by-section port:
759: 
760: **7a. Template refs & non-reactive state:**
761: - `wrapperRef`, `pathRef`, `headerRef`, `contentRef` → `useTemplateRef<HTMLDivElement>()` or `ref<HTMLElement | null>(null)`
762: - `morphTRef`, `aDims`, `dimsRef`, `expandedDimsRef` → plain `let` variables (non-reactive, only used in imperative animation code)
763: - Animation controllers (`morphCtrl`, `pillResizeCtrl`, etc.) → plain `let` variables
764: 
765: **7b. Reactive state:**
766: - `showBody`, `dismissing`, `hovered`, `actionSuccess`, `progressKey` → `ref()`
767: - `dims` (triggers effects) → `ref({ pw: 0, bw: 0, th: 0 })`
768: 
769: **7c. Computed:**
770: - `effectiveTitle`, `effectivePhase`, `effectiveDescription`, `effectiveAction` → `computed()`
771: - `isExpanded`, `isLoading`, `hasDescription`, `hasAction` → `computed()`
772: - `isRight`, `isCenter` → `computed()` reading from `_config`
773: - `useSpring`, `bounceVal`, `showProgress` → `computed()` merging props + presets + config
774: 
775: **7d. `flush()` and `measure()`:**
776: Direct copy from React. These are plain functions that read refs and write to DOM. No React/Vue dependency.
777: 
778: **7e. Morph animation (Phase 2 in React):**
779: `watch(showBody, (val) => { ... })` — drives morph 0→1 on true, 1→0 on false. Uses `animate()` from `motion` package.
780: 
781: **7f. Pre-dismiss timer:**
782: Delegate to `useToastTimer` composable.
783: 
784: **7g. Re-expand on hover:**
785: `watch([hovered, containerHovered], ...)` — if dismissing and now hovered, stop collapse, re-expand.
786: 
787: **7h. Error shake:**
788: `watch(() => props.toast.phase, (newPhase, oldPhase) => { ... })` — trigger shake animation on error transition.
789: 
790: **7i. Landing squish:**
791: `triggerLandingSquish()` — same imperative function, called from morph watchers.
792: 
793: **7j. Header elastic squish:**
794: `watch([showBody, dismissing, actionSuccess], ...)` — squish header during expand/collapse.
795: 
796: **7k. Icon transition:**
797: Replace `<AnimatePresence mode="wait"><motion.div>` with `<Transition mode="out-in">` using CSS opacity/scale.
798: 
799: **7l. Body content (description, action, timestamp):**
800: Replace `<AnimatePresence>` + `<motion.div>` with `<Transition>` components for fade-in/out.
801: 
802: **7m. Dismiss from container:**
803: Instead of `sonnerToast.dismiss(toastId)`, emit `dismiss` event. ToastContainer handles removal from `_toasts`.
804: 
805: **7n. Height reporting:**
806: On mount and after dimension changes, emit `heightChange` so ToastContainer can update stacking.
807: 
808: **Verification**: Component renders correctly, morph animations work, dismiss lifecycle completes.

(Showing lines 694-808 of 1081. Use offset=809 to continue.)
</content>
```

**Tool: read**

**Input:**

<path>/Users/joe/Dev/github/goey-toast/goey-toast-vue/src/composables/useToastTimer.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import { watch, onUnmounted, ref, readonly, type Ref } from 'vue'
2: 
3: const DEFAULT_DISPLAY_DURATION = 4000
4: 
5: export interface UseToastTimerOptions {
6:   duration: Ref<number | undefined>
7:   expandDelayMs: Ref<number>
8:   collapseMs: Ref<number>
9:   paused: Ref<boolean>
10:   enabled: Ref<boolean>
11:   onTimeout: () => void
12: }
13: 
14: export interface UseToastTimerReturn {
15:   progressDuration: Readonly<Ref<number>>
16:   restart: () => void
17: }
18: 
19: export function useToastTimer(options: UseToastTimerOptions): UseToastTimerReturn {
20:   const progressDuration = ref(0)
21:   let remaining: number | null = null
22:   let timerStart = 0
23:   let timer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null
24: 
25:   function clearTimer() {
26:     if (timer !== null) {
27:       clearTimeout(timer)
28:       timer = null
29:     }
30:   }
31: 
32:   function startTimer() {
33:     clearTimer()
34: 
35:     const displayMs = options.duration.value ?? DEFAULT_DISPLAY_DURATION
36:     const fullDelay = displayMs - options.expandDelayMs.value - options.collapseMs.value
37:     progressDuration.value = Math.max(fullDelay, 0)
38:     if (fullDelay <= 0) return
39: 
40:     if (options.paused.value) return
41: 
42:     const delay = remaining ?? fullDelay
43:     timerStart = Date.now()
44: 
45:     timer = setTimeout(() => {
46:       if (options.paused.value) {
47:         const elapsed = Date.now() - timerStart
48:         remaining = Math.max(0, delay - elapsed)
49:         return
50:       }
51:       remaining = null
52:       options.onTimeout()
53:     }, delay)
54:   }
55: 
56:   function pauseTimer() {
57:     if (timer === null) return
58:     const elapsed = Date.now() - timerStart
59:     const delay = remaining ?? progressDuration.value
60:     remaining = Math.max(0, delay - elapsed)
61:     clearTimer()
62:   }
63: 
64:   function resumeTimer() {
65:     if (remaining === null || remaining <= 0) return
66:     startTimer()
67:   }
68: 
69:   function restart() {
70:     remaining = null
71:     if (options.enabled.value && !options.paused.value) {
72:       startTimer()
73:     }
74:   }
75: 
76:   watch(
77:     [options.enabled, options.paused],
78:     ([enabled, paused], [prevEnabled, prevPaused]) => {
79:       if (!enabled) {
80:         clearTimer()
81:         return
82:       }
83: 
84:       if (enabled && !prevEnabled) {
85:         remaining = null
86:         startTimer()
87:         return
88:       }
89: 
90:       if (paused && !prevPaused) {
91:         pauseTimer()
92:       } else if (!paused && prevPaused) {
93:         resumeTimer()
94:       }
95:     },
96:     { immediate: true },
97:   )
98: 
99:   onUnmounted(clearTimer)
100: 
101:   return {
102:     progressDuration: readonly(progressDuration),
103:     restart,
104:   }
105: }

(End of file - total 105 lines)
</content>

<path>/Users/joe/Dev/github/goey-toast/goey-toast-vue/src/gooey-toast.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import { ref, type VNode } from 'vue'
2: import { _config, announce, type AriaLivePoliteness } from './context'
3: import type {
4:   GooeyToastOptions,
5:   GooeyPromiseData,
6:   GooeyToastPhase,
7:   GooeyToastType,
8:   GooeyToastAction,
9:   GooeyToastClassNames,
10:   GooeyToastTimings,
11:   GooeyToastUpdateOptions,
12:   DismissFilter,
13: } from './types'
14: import type { AnimationPresetName } from './presets'
15: 
16: function getAnnouncePoliteness(type: GooeyToastType): AriaLivePoliteness {
17:   return type === 'error' || type === 'warning' ? 'assertive' : 'polite'
18: }
19: 
20: function buildAnnouncementMessage(title: string, description?: VNode | string): string {
21:   if (!description || typeof description !== 'string') return title
22:   return `${title}: ${description}`
23: }
24: 
25: export interface InternalToast {
26:   id: string | number
27:   title: string
28:   type: GooeyToastType
29:   phase: GooeyToastPhase
30:   description?: VNode | string
31:   action?: GooeyToastAction
32:   icon?: VNode | string
33:   duration?: number
34:   classNames?: GooeyToastClassNames
35:   fillColor?: string
36:   borderColor?: string
37:   borderWidth?: number
38:   timing?: GooeyToastTimings
39:   preset?: AnimationPresetName
40:   spring?: boolean
41:   bounce?: number
42:   showProgress?: boolean
43:   showTimestamp?: boolean
44:   _onDismiss?: (id: string | number) => void
45:   _onAutoClose?: (id: string | number) => void
46: }
47: 
48: export const _toasts = ref<InternalToast[]>([])
49: 
50: const _queue: Array<{ id: string | number; type: GooeyToastType; toast: InternalToast }> = []
51: const _autoCloseFlags = new Set<string | number>()
52: const _manualDismissFlags = new Set<string | number>()
53: 
54: export function _markAutoClose(id: string | number) {
55:   _autoCloseFlags.add(id)
56: }
57: 
58: export function _resetQueue() {
59:   _toasts.value = []
60:   _queue.length = 0
61:   _autoCloseFlags.clear()
62:   _manualDismissFlags.clear()
63: }
64: 
65: export function _getMostRecentActiveId(): string | number | undefined {
66:   const toasts = _toasts.value
67:   return toasts.length > 0 ? toasts[toasts.length - 1].id : undefined
68: }
69: 
70: function _processQueue() {
71:   const max = _config.visibleToasts
72:   while (_queue.length > 0 && _toasts.value.length < max) {
73:     const next = _queue.shift()!
74:     _toasts.value.push(next.toast)
75:   }
76: }
77: 
78: function _enqueue(entry: { id: string | number; type: GooeyToastType; toast: InternalToast }): boolean {
79:   const maxQueue = _config.maxQueue
80:   const overflow = _config.queueOverflow
81:   if (_queue.length >= maxQueue) {
82:     if (overflow === 'drop-newest') return false
83:     _queue.shift()
84:   }
85:   _queue.push(entry)
86:   return true
87: }
88: 
89: export function _onToastDismissed(id: string | number) {
90:   const idx = _toasts.value.findIndex(t => t.id === id)
91:   if (idx === -1) return
92: 
93:   const toast = _toasts.value[idx]
94:   _toasts.value.splice(idx, 1)
95: 
96:   const isAutoClose = _autoCloseFlags.has(id) || !_manualDismissFlags.has(id)
97:   if (isAutoClose && toast._onAutoClose) {
98:     try { toast._onAutoClose(id) } catch { /* callback errors must not break queue */ }
99:   }
100:   if (toast._onDismiss) {
101:     try { toast._onDismiss(id) } catch { /* callback errors must not break queue */ }
102:   }
103: 
104:   _autoCloseFlags.delete(id)
105:   _manualDismissFlags.delete(id)
106:   _processQueue()
107: }
108: 
109: function updateGooeyToast(id: string | number, options: GooeyToastUpdateOptions) {
110:   const toast = _toasts.value.find(t => t.id === id)
111:   if (!toast) return
112: 
113:   if (options.title !== undefined) toast.title = options.title
114:   if (options.description !== undefined) toast.description = options.description
115:   if (options.type !== undefined) {
116:     toast.type = options.type
117:     toast.phase = options.type
118:   }
119:   if (options.action !== undefined) toast.action = options.action
120:   if ('icon' in options) toast.icon = options.icon ?? undefined
121:   if (options.showTimestamp !== undefined) toast.showTimestamp = options.showTimestamp
122: 
123:   if (options.title !== undefined) {
124:     announce(
125:       buildAnnouncementMessage(options.title, options.description),
126:       options.type ? getAnnouncePoliteness(options.type) : 'polite',
127:     )
128:   }
129: }
130: 
131: function createGooeyToast(
132:   title: string,
133:   type: GooeyToastType,
134:   options?: GooeyToastOptions,
135: ) {
136:   const toastId = options?.id ?? Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)
137: 
138:   const toast: InternalToast = {
139:     id: toastId,
140:     title,
141:     type,
142:     phase: type,
143:     description: options?.description,
144:     action: options?.action,
145:     icon: options?.icon,
146:     duration: options?.timing?.displayDuration ?? options?.duration,
147:     classNames: options?.classNames,
148:     fillColor: options?.fillColor,
149:     borderColor: options?.borderColor,
150:     borderWidth: options?.borderWidth,
151:     timing: options?.timing,
152:     preset: options?.preset,
153:     spring: options?.spring,
154:     bounce: options?.bounce,
155:     showProgress: options?.showProgress,
156:     showTimestamp: options?.showTimestamp,
157:     _onDismiss: options?.onDismiss,
158:     _onAutoClose: options?.onAutoClose,
159:   }
160: 
161:   announce(
162:     buildAnnouncementMessage(title, options?.description),
163:     getAnnouncePoliteness(type),
164:   )
165: 
166:   if (_toasts.value.length < _config.visibleToasts) {
167:     _toasts.value.push(toast)
168:   } else {
169:     _enqueue({ id: toastId, type, toast })
170:   }
171: 
172:   return toastId
173: }
174: 
175: function dismissGooeyToast(idOrFilter?: string | number | DismissFilter) {
176:   if (idOrFilter != null && typeof idOrFilter === 'object') {
177:     const filterTypes = Array.isArray(idOrFilter.type) ? idOrFilter.type : [idOrFilter.type]
178:     const typesSet = new Set<GooeyToastType>(filterTypes)
179: 
180:     for (let i = _queue.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
181:       if (typesSet.has(_queue[i].type)) {
182:         _queue.splice(i, 1)
183:       }
184:     }
185: 
186:     const toRemove = _toasts.value.filter(t => typesSet.has(t.type))
187:     for (const t of toRemove) {
188:       _manualDismissFlags.add(t.id)
189:       _onToastDismissed(t.id)
190:     }
191:   } else if (idOrFilter != null) {
192:     const qIdx = _queue.findIndex(q => q.id === idOrFilter)
193:     if (qIdx !== -1) {
194:       _queue.splice(qIdx, 1)
195:       return
196:     }
197:     _manualDismissFlags.add(idOrFilter)
198:     _onToastDismissed(idOrFilter)
199:   } else {
200:     for (const t of _toasts.value) {
201:       _manualDismissFlags.add(t.id)
202:     }
203:     _queue.length = 0
204:     const all = [..._toasts.value]
205:     _toasts.value = []
206:     for (const t of all) {
207:       const isAutoClose = _autoCloseFlags.has(t.id) || !_manualDismissFlags.has(t.id)
208:       if (isAutoClose && t._onAutoClose) {
209:         try { t._onAutoClose(t.id) } catch { /* noop */ }
210:       }
211:       if (t._onDismiss) {
212:         try { t._onDismiss(t.id) } catch { /* noop */ }
213:       }
214:       _autoCloseFlags.delete(t.id)
215:       _manualDismissFlags.delete(t.id)
216:     }
217:   }
218: }
219: 
220: export const gooeyToast = Object.assign(
221:   (title: string, options?: GooeyToastOptions) =>
222:     createGooeyToast(title, 'default', options),
223:   {
224:     success: (title: string, options?: GooeyToastOptions) =>
225:       createGooeyToast(title, 'success', options),
226:     error: (title: string, options?: GooeyToastOptions) =>
227:       createGooeyToast(title, 'error', options),
228:     warning: (title: string, options?: GooeyToastOptions) =>
229:       createGooeyToast(title, 'warning', options),
230:     info: (title: string, options?: GooeyToastOptions) =>
231:       createGooeyToast(title, 'info', options),
232:     promise: <T,>(promise: Promise<T>, data: GooeyPromiseData<T>) => {
233:       const id = Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)
234: 
235:       announce(buildAnnouncementMessage(data.loading, data.description?.loading), 'polite')
236: 
237:       const toast: InternalToast = {
238:         id,
239:         title: data.loading,
240:         type: 'info',
241:         phase: 'loading',
242:         description: data.description?.loading,
243:         classNames: data.classNames,
244:         fillColor: data.fillColor,
245:         borderColor: data.borderColor,
246:         borderWidth: data.borderWidth,
247:         timing: data.timing,
248:         preset: data.preset,
249:         spring: data.spring,
250:         bounce: data.bounce,
251:         showTimestamp: data.showTimestamp,
252:         _onDismiss: data.onDismiss,
253:         _onAutoClose: data.onAutoClose,
254:       }
255: 
256:       if (_toasts.value.length < _config.visibleToasts) {
257:         _toasts.value.push(toast)
258:       } else {
259:         _enqueue({ id, type: 'info', toast })
260:       }
261: 
262:       promise
263:         .then((result) => {
264:           const existing = _toasts.value.find(t => t.id === id)
265:           if (!existing) return
266:           const desc = typeof data.description?.success === 'function'
267:             ? data.description.success(result)
268:             : data.description?.success
269:           const resolvedTitle = typeof data.success === 'function'
270:             ? data.success(result)
271:             : data.success
272:           existing.title = resolvedTitle
273:           existing.description = desc
274:           existing.action = data.action?.success
275:           existing.phase = 'success'
276:           existing.type = 'success'
277:           announce(buildAnnouncementMessage(resolvedTitle, desc), 'polite')
278:         })
279:         .catch((err) => {
280:           const existing = _toasts.value.find(t => t.id === id)
281:           if (!existing) return
282:           const desc = typeof data.description?.error === 'function'
283:             ? data.description.error(err)
284:             : data.description?.error
285:           const resolvedTitle = typeof data.error === 'function' ? data.error(err) : data.error
286:           existing.title = resolvedTitle
287:           existing.description = desc
288:           existing.action = data.action?.error
289:           existing.phase = 'error'
290:           existing.type = 'error'
291:           announce(buildAnnouncementMessage(resolvedTitle, desc), 'assertive')
292:         })
293: 
294:       return id
295:     },
296:     dismiss: dismissGooeyToast,
297:     update: updateGooeyToast,
298:   },
299: )

7: import { getGooeyPosition, getGooeyDir, getGooeySpring, getGooeyBounce, getGooeySwipeToDismiss, getGooeyTheme, getGooeyShowProgress, getGooeyCloseButton, subscribeContainerHovered, getContainerHovered } from '../context'
8: import { DefaultIcon, SuccessIcon, ErrorIcon, WarningIcon, InfoIcon, SpinnerIcon } from '../icons'
9: import { usePrefersReducedMotion } from '../usePrefersReducedMotion'
10: import { styles } from './gooey-styles'
11: 
12: // SSR-safe useLayoutEffect: avoids React warning when rendering on the server.
13: // Falls back to useEffect during SSR (no DOM to measure anyway).
14: const useIsomorphicLayoutEffect =
15:   typeof window !== 'undefined' ? useLayoutEffectOrig : useEffect
16: 
17: export interface GooeyToastProps {
18:   title: string
19:   description?: ReactNode
20:   type: GooeyToastType
21:   action?: GooeyToastAction
22:   icon?: ReactNode
23:   phase: GooeyToastPhase
24:   classNames?: GooeyToastClassNames
25:   fillColor?: string
26:   borderColor?: string
27:   borderWidth?: number
28:   timing?: GooeyToastTimings
29:   preset?: AnimationPresetName
30:   spring?: boolean
31:   bounce?: number
32:   showProgress?: boolean
33:   showTimestamp?: boolean
34:   toastId?: string | number
35: }
36: 
37: const phaseIconMap: Record<Exclude<GooeyToastPhase, 'loading'>, FC<{ size?: number; className?: string }>> = {
38:   default: DefaultIcon,
39:   success: SuccessIcon,
40:   error: ErrorIcon,
41:   warning: WarningIcon,
42:   info: InfoIcon,
43: }
44: 
45: const titleColorMap: Record<GooeyToastPhase, string> = {
46:   loading: styles.titleLoading,
47:   default: styles.titleDefault,
48:   success: styles.titleSuccess,
49:   error: styles.titleError,
50:   warning: styles.titleWarning,
51:   info: styles.titleInfo,
52: }
53: 
54: const actionColorMap: Record<GooeyToastPhase, string> = {
55:   loading: styles.actionInfo,
56:   default: styles.actionDefault,
57:   success: styles.actionSuccess,
58:   error: styles.actionError,
59:   warning: styles.actionWarning,
60:   info: styles.actionInfo,
61: }
62: 
63: const progressColorMap: Record<GooeyToastPhase, string> = {
64:   loading: styles.progressInfo,
65:   default: styles.progressDefault,
66:   success: styles.progressSuccess,
67:   error: styles.progressError,
68:   warning: styles.progressWarning,
69:   info: styles.progressInfo,
70: }
71: 
72: const PH = 34 // pill height constant
73: const DEFAULT_DISPLAY_DURATION = 4000
74: 
75: // Squish spring config — scales mass with morph duration so feel stays consistent
76: // bounce 0.0 = heavily damped (subtle), 0.8 = very bouncy (dramatic)
77: const DEFAULT_EXPAND_DUR = 0.6
78: const DEFAULT_COLLAPSE_DUR = 0.9
79: function squishSpring(durationSec: number, defaultDur: number, bounce = 0.4) {
80:   const scale = durationSec / defaultDur
81:   // Map bounce (0-0.8) to stiffness (200-550) and damping (24-8)
82:   const stiffness = 200 + bounce * 437.5
83:   const damping = 24 - bounce * 20
84:   const mass = 0.7 * scale
85:   return { type: 'spring' as const, stiffness, damping, mass }
86: }
87: 
88: /**
89:  * Singleton MutationObserver registry — one observer per <ol> element shared
90:  * across all GooeyToast instances mounted under that list. Each toast registers
91:  * its own callback; the shared observer batches mutations via rAF and invokes
92:  * every registered callback once per frame.
93:  */
94: const observerRegistry = new Map<Element, {
95:   observer: MutationObserver
96:   callbacks: Set<() => void>
97: }>()
98: 
99: function registerSonnerObserver(ol: Element, callback: () => void) {
100:   let entry = observerRegistry.get(ol)
101:   if (!entry) {
102:     const callbacks = new Set<() => void>()
103:     let applying = false
104:     const observer = new MutationObserver(() => {
105:       if (applying) return
106:       applying = true
107:       requestAnimationFrame(() => {
108:         callbacks.forEach(cb => cb())
109:         requestAnimationFrame(() => { applying = false })
110:       })
111:     })
112:     observer.observe(ol, {
113:       attributes: true,
114:       attributeFilter: ['style', 'data-visible'],
115:       subtree: true,
116:       childList: true,
117:     })
118:     entry = { observer, callbacks }
119:     observerRegistry.set(ol, entry)
120:   }
121:   entry.callbacks.add(callback)
122:   return () => {
123:     entry!.callbacks.delete(callback)
124:     if (entry!.callbacks.size === 0) {
125:       entry!.observer.disconnect()
126:       observerRegistry.delete(ol)
127:     }
128:   }
129: }
130: 
131: /**
132:  * Recalculates CSS variables on all sibling toast <li> elements.
133:  *
134:  * Sonner measures height once on mount (~34px pill) and never re-measures
135:  * toast.custom() content. This function corrects --initial-height and --offset
136:  * so our morphing toasts stack correctly.
137:  *
138:  * When expanded (hovered), CSS transitions on [data-expanded="true"] are
139:  * overridden (GooeyToast.css) so writing these values takes effect instantly.
140:  *
141:  * DOM order: oldest toast at index 0, newest (front) at index n-1.
142:  * Front toast has --offset: 0; each older toast accumulates the heights of all
143:  * newer toasts plus the gap between them.
144:  */
145: function syncSonnerHeights(
146:   wrapperEl: HTMLElement | null,
147:   includeOffsets = false,
148: ) {
149:   if (!wrapperEl) return
150:   const li = wrapperEl.closest('[data-sonner-toast]') as HTMLElement | null
151:   if (!li?.parentElement) return
152: 
153:   const ol = li.parentElement
154:   const toasts = Array.from(
155:     ol.querySelectorAll(':scope > [data-sonner-toast]')
156:   ) as HTMLElement[]
157: 
158:   if (toasts.length === 0) return
159: 
160:   // Measure actual rendered height of each toast's content wrapper.
161:   // Invisible toasts (beyond Sonner's visibleToasts limit) get height 0
162:   // so they don't create a gap in the layout or affect offset calculations.
163:   const heights = toasts.map(t => {
164:     if (t.getAttribute('data-visible') === 'false') return 0
165:     const content = t.firstElementChild as HTMLElement | null
166:     const h = content ? content.getBoundingClientRect().height : 0
167:     return h > 0 ? h : PH
168:   })
169: 
170:   // When expanded (hovered), temporarily kill CSS transitions so offset
171:   // corrections apply in this paint — prevents overlap flash. The CSS shortens
172:   // transitions to 150ms (GooeyToast.css) for Sonner's own changes, but our
173:   // corrections must be truly instant since getBoundingClientRect above forces
174:   // a layout that "locks in" stale values.
175:   const isExpanded = includeOffsets && toasts[0]?.getAttribute('data-expanded') === 'true'
176:   if (isExpanded) {
177:     for (const t of toasts) t.style.setProperty('transition', 'none', 'important')
178:   }
179: 
180:   // Always update --initial-height (the CSS height transition target).
181:   for (let i = 0; i < toasts.length; i++) {
182:     toasts[i].style.setProperty('--initial-height', `${heights[i]}px`)
183:   }
184: 
185:   if (!includeOffsets) {
186:     if (isExpanded) {
187:       for (const t of toasts) t.style.removeProperty('transition')
188:     }
189:     return
190:   }
191: 
192:   const gapStr = getComputedStyle(ol).getPropertyValue('--gap').trim()
193:   const gap = parseFloat(gapStr) || 14
194: 
195:   let runningOffset = 0
196:   for (let i = toasts.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
197:     // Invisible toasts: collapse to base position, don't contribute to offset
198:     if (toasts[i].getAttribute('data-visible') === 'false') {
199:       toasts[i].style.setProperty('--offset', '0px')
200:       continue
201:     }
202:     toasts[i].style.setProperty('--offset', `${runningOffset}px`)
203:     if (i > 0) {
204:       runningOffset += heights[i] + gap
205:     }
206:   }
207: 
208:   if (isExpanded) {
209:     // Force reflow so browser applies corrected values without transitions,
210:     // then restore transitions for Sonner's own future animations.
211:     void ol.offsetHeight
212:     for (const t of toasts) t.style.removeProperty('transition')
213:   }
214: }
215: 
216: /**
217:  * Simple last-result cache for pure functions called every animation frame.
218:  * Avoids recomputing the SVG path string when flush() is called multiple
219:  * times with identical parameters (e.g. syncSonnerHeights after morph update).
220:  */
221: function memoizePath(fn: (pw: number, bw: number, th: number, t: number) => string) {
222:   let lastArgs: [number, number, number, number] | null = null
223:   let lastResult = ''
224:   return (pw: number, bw: number, th: number, t: number): string => {
225:     if (lastArgs && lastArgs[0] === pw && lastArgs[1] === bw && lastArgs[2] === th && lastArgs[3] === t) {
226:       return lastResult
227:     }
228:     lastResult = fn(pw, bw, th, t)
229:     lastArgs = [pw, bw, th, t]
230:     return lastResult
231:   }
232: }
233: 
234: /**
235:  * Parametric morph path: pill lobe stays constant, body grows from underneath.
236:  * t=0 → pure pill, t=1 → full organic blob.
237:  */
238: function morphPathRaw(pw: number, bw: number, th: number, t: number): string {
239:   const pr = PH / 2
240:   const pillW = Math.min(pw, bw)
241: 
242:   const bodyH = PH + (th - PH) * t
243: 
244:   // Pure pill when t is zero or body too small for proper rounded corners
245:   if (t <= 0 || bodyH - PH < 8) {
246:     return [
247:       `M 0,${pr}`,
248:       `A ${pr},${pr} 0 0 1 ${pr},0`,
249:       `H ${pillW - pr}`,
250:       `A ${pr},${pr} 0 0 1 ${pillW},${pr}`,
251:       `A ${pr},${pr} 0 0 1 ${pillW - pr},${PH}`,
252:       `H ${pr}`,
253:       `A ${pr},${pr} 0 0 1 0,${pr}`,
254:       `Z`,
255:     ].join(' ')
256:   }
257: 
258:   const curve = 14 * t
259:   const cr = Math.min(16, (bodyH - PH) * 0.45)
260:   const bodyW = pillW + (bw - pillW) * t
261:   const bodyTop = PH - curve
262:   const qEndX = Math.min(pillW + curve, bodyW - cr)
263: 
264:   return [
265:     `M 0,${pr}`,
266:     `A ${pr},${pr} 0 0 1 ${pr},0`,
267:     `H ${pillW - pr}`,
268:     `A ${pr},${pr} 0 0 1 ${pillW},${pr}`,
269:     `L ${pillW},${bodyTop}`,
270:     `Q ${pillW},${bodyTop + curve} ${qEndX},${bodyTop + curve}`,
271:     `H ${bodyW - cr}`,
272:     `A ${cr},${cr} 0 0 1 ${bodyW},${bodyTop + curve + cr}`,
273:     `L ${bodyW},${bodyH - cr}`,
274:     `A ${cr},${cr} 0 0 1 ${bodyW - cr},${bodyH}`,
275:     `H ${cr}`,
276:     `A ${cr},${cr} 0 0 1 0,${bodyH - cr}`,
277:     `Z`,
278:   ].join(' ')
279: }
280: 
281: /**
282:  * Centered morph path: pill centered on top, body grows symmetrically below.
283:  * t=0 → pure pill (centered), t=1 → full blob with pill centered on top.
284:  */
285: function morphPathCenterRaw(pw: number, bw: number, th: number, t: number): string {
286:   const pr = PH / 2
287:   const pillW = Math.min(pw, bw)
288: 
289:   // Pill is ALWAYS centered at the final body width position
290:   const pillOffset = (bw - pillW) / 2
291: 
292:   // Pure pill when t is zero or body too small
293:   if (t <= 0 || PH + (th - PH) * t - PH < 8) {
294:     return [
295:       `M ${pillOffset},${pr}`,
296:       `A ${pr},${pr} 0 0 1 ${pillOffset + pr},0`,
297:       `H ${pillOffset + pillW - pr}`,
298:       `A ${pr},${pr} 0 0 1 ${pillOffset + pillW},${pr}`,
299:       `A ${pr},${pr} 0 0 1 ${pillOffset + pillW - pr},${PH}`,
300:       `H ${pillOffset + pr}`,
301:       `A ${pr},${pr} 0 0 1 ${pillOffset},${pr}`,
302:       `Z`,
303:     ].join(' ')
304:   }
305: 
306:   const bodyH = PH + (th - PH) * t
307:   const curve = 14 * t
308:   const cr = Math.min(16, (bodyH - PH) * 0.45)
309:   const bodyTop = PH - curve
310: 
311:   // Body grows symmetrically outward from pill center
312:   const bodyCenter = bw / 2
313:   const halfBodyW = (pillW / 2) + ((bw - pillW) / 2) * t  // grows from pillW/2 to bw/2
314:   const bodyLeft = bodyCenter - halfBodyW
315:   const bodyRight = bodyCenter + halfBodyW
316: 
317:   // Q curve endpoints: body edges meet pill edges with organic curves
318:   const qLeftX = Math.max(bodyLeft + cr, pillOffset - curve)
319:   const qRightX = Math.min(bodyRight - cr, pillOffset + pillW + curve)
320: 
321:   return [
322:     // Start at left side of pill
323:     `M ${pillOffset},${pr}`,
324:     // Pill top-left arc
325:     `A ${pr},${pr} 0 0 1 ${pillOffset + pr},0`,
326:     // Top edge of pill
327:     `H ${pillOffset + pillW - pr}`,
328:     // Pill top-right arc
329:     `A ${pr},${pr} 0 0 1 ${pillOffset + pillW},${pr}`,
330:     // Right side down to body junction
331:     `L ${pillOffset + pillW},${bodyTop}`,
332:     // Right organic curve from pill to body
333:     `Q ${pillOffset + pillW},${bodyTop + curve} ${qRightX},${bodyTop + curve}`,
334:     // Right side of body
335:     `H ${bodyRight - cr}`,
336:     // Body top-right corner
337:     `A ${cr},${cr} 0 0 1 ${bodyRight},${bodyTop + curve + cr}`,
338:     // Right edge down
339:     `L ${bodyRight},${bodyH - cr}`,
340:     // Body bottom-right corner
341:     `A ${cr},${cr} 0 0 1 ${bodyRight - cr},${bodyH}`,
342:     // Bottom edge
343:     `H ${bodyLeft + cr}`,
344:     // Body bottom-left corner
345:     `A ${cr},${cr} 0 0 1 ${bodyLeft},${bodyH - cr}`,
346:     // Left edge up
347:     `L ${bodyLeft},${bodyTop + curve + cr}`,
348:     // Body top-left corner
349:     `A ${cr},${cr} 0 0 1 ${bodyLeft + cr},${bodyTop + curve}`,
350:     // Left side of body
351:     `H ${qLeftX}`,
352:     // Left organic curve from body to pill
353:     `Q ${pillOffset},${bodyTop + curve} ${pillOffset},${bodyTop}`,
354:     // Close back to start
355:     `Z`,
356:   ].join(' ')
357: }
358: 
359: const morphPath = memoizePath(morphPathRaw)
360: const morphPathCenter = memoizePath(morphPathCenterRaw)
361: 
362: // Smooth easing curve for non-spring animations
363: const SMOOTH_EASE = [0.4, 0, 0.2, 1] as const
364: 
365: export const GooeyToast: FC<GooeyToastProps> = ({
366:   title,
367:   description,
368:   action,
369:   icon,
370:   phase,
371:   classNames,
372:   fillColor: fillColorProp,
373:   borderColor,
374:   borderWidth,
375:   timing,
376:   preset,
377:   spring: springProp,
378:   bounce: bounceProp,
379:   showTimestamp = true,
380:   showProgress: showProgressProp,
381:   toastId,
382: }) => {
383:   const theme = getGooeyTheme()
384:   const closeButtonSetting = getGooeyCloseButton()
385:   const showCloseButton = closeButtonSetting !== false
386:   const fillColor = fillColorProp ?? (theme === 'dark' ? '#1a1a1a' : '#ffffff')
387:   const position = getGooeyPosition()
388:   const dir = getGooeyDir()
389:   const posIsRight = position?.includes('right') ?? false
390:   const isCenter = position?.includes('center') ?? false
391:   // In RTL, left-positioned toasts visually behave like right-positioned ones and vice versa
392:   const isRight = dir === 'rtl' ? (isCenter ? false : !posIsRight) : posIsRight
393:   const prefersReducedMotion = usePrefersReducedMotion()
394:   // Explicit props > per-toast preset > global context values > defaults
395:   const presetConfig = preset ? animationPresets[preset] : undefined
396:   const useSpring = springProp ?? presetConfig?.spring ?? getGooeySpring()
397:   const bounceVal = bounceProp ?? presetConfig?.bounce ?? getGooeyBounce() ?? 0.4
398:   const showProgress = showProgressProp ?? getGooeyShowProgress()
399: 
400:   // Action success override state

(Showing lines 1-400 of 1350. Use offset=401 to continue.)

[Project README: /Users/joe/Dev/github/goey-toast/goey-toast-react/README.md]
# gooey-toast

[![gooey-toast](https://goey-toast.vercel.app/og-image.png)](https://goey-toast.vercel.app)

**[Live Demo & Docs](https://goey-toast.vercel.app)**

## Features

- Organic blob morph animation (pill &rarr; blob &rarr; pill)
- Five toast types: default, success, error, warning, info
- Promise toasts with loading &rarr; success/error transitions
- Action buttons with optional success label morph-back
- Description body supporting strings and React components
- Configurable display duration and bounce intensity
- Custom fill color, border color, and border width
- CSS class overrides via `classNames` prop
- 6 positions with automatic horizontal mirroring for right-side positions
- Center positions with symmetric morph animation
- Hover pause: hovering an expanded toast pauses the dismiss timer
- Hover re-expand: hovering a collapsed pill re-expands the toast
- Pre-dismiss collapse animation
- In-place toast updates via `gooeyToast.update()`
- Dismiss by type filter: `gooeyToast.dismiss({ type: 'error' })`
- Dark mode and RTL layout support
- Animation presets: smooth, bouncy, subtle, snappy
- Timestamp display on expanded toasts with optional `showTimestamp` toggle
- Close button with configurable position (`top-left` / `top-right`)
- Countdown progress bar with hover-pause and re-expand
- Keyboard dismiss (Escape) and swipe-to-dismiss on mobile
- Toast queue with configurable overflow strategy
- Dismiss callbacks: `onDismiss` and `onAutoClose`

## Installation

```bash
npm install goey-toast
```

### shadcn/ui

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add https://goey-toast.vercel.app/r/goey-toaster.json
```

This installs a thin wrapper component at `components/ui/goey-toaster.tsx` and auto-installs the `goey-toast` and `framer-motion` packages.

### Peer Dependencies

goey-toast requires the following peer dependencies:

```bash
npm install react react-dom framer-motion
```

| Package        | Version    |
| -------------- | ---------- |
| react          | >= 18.0.0  |
| react-dom      | >= 18.0.0  |
| framer-motion  | >= 10.0.0  |

### CSS Import (Required)

You **must** import the goey-toast stylesheet for the component to render correctly:

```tsx
import 'goey-toast/styles.css'
```

Add this import once in your app's entry point (e.g., `main.tsx` or `App.tsx`). Without it, toasts will appear unstyled.

## Quick Start

```tsx
import { GooeyToaster, gooeyToast } from 'goey-toast'
import 'goey-toast/styles.css'

function App() {
  return (
    <>
      <GooeyToaster position="bottom-right" />
      <button onClick={() => gooeyToast.success('Saved!')}>
        Save
      </button>
    </>
  )
}
```

## API Reference

### `gooeyToast` Methods

```ts
gooeyToast(title, options?)              // default (neutral)
gooeyToast.success(title, options?)      // green
gooeyToast.error(title, options?)        // red
gooeyToast.warning(title, options?)      // yellow
gooeyToast.info(title, options?)         // blue
gooeyToast.promise(promise, data)        // loading -> success/error
gooeyToast.update(id, options)           // update an existing toast in-place
gooeyToast.dismiss(idOrFilter?)          // dismiss one, by type, or all toasts
```

#### `gooeyToast.update(id, options)`

Updates an existing toast in-place without removing and re-creating it.

```tsx
const id = gooeyToast('Uploading...', {
  icon: <SpinnerIcon />,
})

// Later, update the toast
gooeyToast.update(id, {
  title: 'Upload complete',
  type: 'success',
  description: '3 files uploaded.',
  icon: null, // clears the custom icon
})
```

**`GooeyToastUpdateOptions`:**

| Option        | Type              | Description                   |
| ------------- | ----------------- | ----------------------------- |
| `title`       | `string`          | New title text                |
| `description` | `ReactNode`       | New body content              |
| `type`        | `GooeyToastType`   | Change the toast type/color   |
| `action`      | `GooeyToastAction` | New action button             |
| `icon`        | `ReactNode \| null` | Custom icon (pass `null` to clear) |

#### `gooeyToast.dismiss(idOrFilter?)`

Dismiss a single toast by ID, all toasts of a given type, or all toasts at once.

```ts
// Dismiss a specific toast
gooeyToast.dismiss(toastId)

// Dismiss all error toasts
gooeyToast.dismiss({ type: 'error' })

// Dismiss multiple types
gooeyToast.dismiss({ type: ['error', 'warning'] })

// Dismiss all toasts
gooeyToast.dismiss()
```

### `GooeyToastOptions`

Options passed as the second argument to `gooeyToast()` and type-specific methods.

| Option        | Type                 | Description                        |
| ------------- | -------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| `description` | `ReactNode`          | Body content (string or component) |
| `action`      | `GooeyToastAction`    | Action button configuration        |
| `icon`        | `ReactNode`          | Custom icon override               |
| `duration`    | `number`             | Display duration in ms             |
| `id`          | `string \| number`   | Unique toast identifier            |
| `classNames`  | `GooeyToastClassNames`| CSS class overrides                |
| `fillColor`   | `string`             | Background color of the blob       |
| `borderColor` | `string`             | Border color of the blob           |
| `borderWidth` | `number`             | Border width in px (default 1.5)   |
| `timing`      | `GooeyToastTimings`   | Animation timing overrides         |
| `spring`      | `boolean`            | Enable spring/bounce animations (default `true`) |
| `bounce`      | `number`             | Spring intensity from `0.05` (subtle) to `0.8` (dramatic), default `0.4` |
| `showTimestamp` | `boolean`         | Show/hide timestamp in toast header/body (default `true`) |
| `showProgress`| `boolean`            | Show countdown progress bar                      |
| `onDismiss`   | `(id) => void`       | Called when toast is dismissed (any reason)       |
| `onAutoClose` | `(id) => void`       | Called only on timer-based auto-dismiss           |
| `preset`      | `AnimationPresetName`| Animation preset (`'smooth'`, `'bouncy'`, `'subtle'`, `'snappy'`) |

### `GooeyToastAction`

| Property       | Type       | Required | Description                                  |
| -------------- | ---------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `label`        | `string`   | Yes      | Button text                                  |
| `onClick`      | `() => void` | Yes   | Click handler                                |
| `successLabel` | `string`   | No       | Label shown after click (morphs back to pill)|

### `GooeyToastTimings`

Fine-tune animation speeds per toast.

| Property           | Type     | Default | Description                          |
| ------------------ | -------- | ------- | ------------------------------------ |
| `displayDuration`  | `number` | 4000    | Milliseconds toast stays expanded    |

### `GooeyToastClassNames`

Override styles for any part of the toast.

| Key             | Target           |
| --------------- | ---------------- |
| `wrapper`       | Outer container  |
| `content`       | Content area     |
| `header`        | Icon + title row |
| `title`         | Title text       |
| `icon`          | Icon wrapper     |
| `description`   | Body text        |
| `actionWrapper` | Button container |
| `actionButton`  | Action button    |

### `GooeyToasterProps`

Props for the `<GooeyToaster />` component.

| Prop         | Type                                  | Default          | Description                                   |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------- | ---------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `position`   | `'top-left' \| 'top-center' \| 'top-right' \| 'bottom-left' \| 'bottom-center' \| 'bottom-right'` | `'bottom-right'` | Toast position |
| `duration`   | `number`                              | --               | Default display duration in ms                |
| `gap`        | `number`                              | `14`             | Gap between stacked toasts (px)               |
| `offset`     | `number \| string`                    | `'24px'`         | Distance from screen edge                     |
| `theme`      | `'light' \| 'dark'`                   | `'light'`        | Color theme                                   |
| `toastOptions` | `Partial<ExternalToast>`            | --               | Default options passed to Sonner              |
| `spring`     | `boolean`                             | `true`           | Enable spring/bounce animations globally      |
| `bounce`     | `number`                              | `0.4`            | Spring intensity: `0.05` (subtle) to `0.8` (dramatic) |
| `preset`     | `AnimationPresetName`                 | --               | Animation preset for all toasts               |
| `closeOnEscape` | `boolean`                          | `true`           | Dismiss most recent toast on Escape key       |
| `closeButton`  | `boolean \| 'top-left' \| 'top-right'` | `false`       | Show close button on hover                    |
| `showProgress` | `boolean`                           | `false`          | Show countdown progress bar on all toasts     |
| `maxQueue`   | `number`                              | `Infinity`       | Maximum queued toasts                         |
| `queueOverflow` | `'drop-oldest' \| 'drop-newest'`   | `'drop-oldest'`  | Queue overflow strategy                       |
| `dir`        | `'ltr' \| 'rtl'`                     | `'ltr'`          | Layout direction                              |
| `swipeToDismiss` | `boolean`                         | `true`           | Enable swipe-to-dismiss on mobile             |

### `GooeyPromiseData<T>`

Configuration for `gooeyToast.promise()`.

| Property      | Type                                          | Required | Description                                    |
| ------------- | --------------------------------------------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `loading`     | `string`                                      | Yes      | Title shown during loading                     |
| `success`     | `string \| ((data: T) => string)`             | Yes      | Title on success (static or derived from result)|
| `error`       | `string \| ((error: unknown) => string)`      | Yes      | Title on error (static or derived from error)  |
| `description` | `object`                                      | No       | Per-phase descriptions (see below)             |
| `action`      | `object`                                      | No       | Per-phase action buttons (see below)           |
| `classNames`  | `GooeyToastClassNames`                         | No       | CSS class overrides                            |
| `fillColor`   | `string`                                      | No       | Background color of the blob                   |
| `borderColor` | `string`                                      | No       | Border color of the blob                       |
| `borderWidth` | `number`                                      | No       | Border width in px                             |
| `timing`      | `GooeyToastTimings`                            | No       | Animation timing overrides                     |
| `spring`      | `boolean`                                     | No       | Enable spring/bounce animations (default `true`) |
| `bounce`      | `number`                                      | No       | Spring intensity: `0.05` (subtle) to `0.8` (dramatic), default `0.4` |
| `onDismiss`   | `(id: string \| number) => void`              | No       | Called when toast is dismissed (any reason)       |
| `onAutoClose` | `(id: string \| number) => void`              | No       | Called only on timer-based auto-dismiss           |

**`description` sub-fields:**

| Key       | Type                                             |
| --------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `loading` | `ReactNode`                                      |
| `success` | `ReactNode \| ((data: T) => ReactNode)`          |
| `error`   | `ReactNode \| ((error: unknown) => ReactNode)`   |

**`action` sub-fields:**

| Key       | Type              |
| --------- | ----------------- |
| `success` | `GooeyToastAction` |
| `error`   | `GooeyToastAction` |

## Usage Examples

### Description

```tsx
gooeyToast.error('Payment failed', {
  description: 'Your card was declined. Please try again.',
})
```

### Custom React Component as Description

```tsx
gooeyToast.success('Deployment complete', {
  description: (
    <div style={{ display: 'flex', flexDirection: 'column', gap: 10 }}>
      <div>
        <span>Environment:</span> <strong>Production</strong>
      </div>
      <div>
        <span>Branch:</span> <strong>main @ 3f8a2c1</strong>
      </div>
    </div>
  ),
})
```

### Action Button with Success Label

```tsx
gooeyToast.info('Share link ready', {
  description: 'Your link has been generated.',
  action: {
    label: 'Copy to Clipboard',
    onClick: () => navigator.clipboard.writeText(url),
    successLabel: 'Copied!',
  },
})
```

### Promise Toast

```tsx
gooeyToast.promise(saveData(), {
  loading: 'Saving...',
  success: 'Changes saved',
  error: 'Something went wrong',
  description: {
    success: 'All changes have been synced.',
    error: 'Please try again later.',
  },
  action: {
    error: {
      label: 'Retry',
      onClick: () => retry(),
    },
  },
})
```

### Custom Styling

```tsx
gooeyToast.success('Styled!', {
  fillColor: '#1a1a2e',
  borderColor: '#333',
  borderWidth: 2,
  classNames: {
    wrapper: 'my-wrapper',
    title: 'my-title',
    description: 'my-desc',
    actionButton: 'my-btn',
  },
})
```

### Display Duration

```tsx
gooeyToast.success('Saved', {
  description: 'Your changes have been synced.',
  timing: { displayDuration: 5000 },
})
```

### Disabling Spring Animations

Disable bounce/spring animations for a cleaner, more subtle look:

```tsx
// Per-toast: disable spring for this toast only
gooeyToast.success('Saved', {
  description: 'Your changes have been synced.',
  spring: false,
})

// Globally: disable spring for all toasts
<GooeyToaster spring={false} />
```

When `spring` is `false`, all spring-based animations (landing squish, blob squish, morph transitions, pill resize, header squish) use smooth ease-in-out curves instead. Error shake animations still work regardless of this setting.

### Bounce Intensity

Control how dramatic the spring effect feels with a single `bounce` value:

```tsx
// Subtle, barely-there spring
gooeyToast.success('Saved', { bounce: 0.1 })

// Default feel
gooeyToast.success('Saved', { bounce: 0.4 })

// Jelly mode
gooeyToast.success('Saved', { bounce: 0.8 })

// Set globally via GooeyToaster
<GooeyToaster bounce={0.6} />
```

The `bounce` value (0.05 to 0.8) controls spring stiffness, damping, and squish magnitude together so you get a consistent feel from one number.

### Dark Mode

```tsx
<GooeyToaster theme="dark" />
```

### RTL Support

```tsx
<GooeyToaster dir="rtl" />
```

### Animation Presets

Four built-in presets: `smooth`, `bouncy`, `subtle`, `snappy`. Apply per-toast or globally:

```tsx
gooeyToast.success('Saved', { preset: 'bouncy' })

// Or globally
<GooeyToaster preset="smooth" />
```

### Progress Bar

Show a countdown progress bar on toasts:

```tsx
gooeyToast.success('Saved', { showProgress: true })

// Or enable globally
<GooeyToaster showProgress />
```

### Keyboard Shortcuts

Press **Escape** to dismiss the most recent toast. Enabled by default; disable with `closeOnEscape={false}`.

### Swipe to Dismiss

On mobile, swipe toasts to dismiss them. Enabled by default; disable with `swipeToDismiss={false}`.

### Close Button

Show a close button on hover. Position it `top-left` (default) or `top-right`:

```tsx
<GooeyToaster closeButton />
<GooeyToaster closeButton="top-left" />
<GooeyToaster closeButton="top-right" />
```

The close button inherits the toast's border and fill color styling. Hidden during the loading phase of promise toasts.

### Hiding Timestamps

Hide the timestamp from toasts:

```tsx
gooeyToast.success('Saved', { showTimestamp: false })
```

## Exports

```ts
// Components
export { GooeyToaster } from 'goey-toast'

// Toast function
export { gooeyToast } from 'goey-toast'

// Animation presets
export { animationPresets } from 'goey-toast'

// Types
export type {
  GooeyToastOptions,
  GooeyPromiseData,
  GooeyToasterProps,
  GooeyToastAction,
  GooeyToastClassNames,
  GooeyToastTimings,
  GooeyToastUpdateOptions,
  DismissFilter,
  AnimationPreset,
  AnimationPresetName,
} from 'goey-toast'
```

## Browser Support

goey-toast works in all modern browsers that support:

- CSS Modules
- SVG path animations
- ResizeObserver
- `framer-motion` (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)

## See Also

- **[gooey-search-tabs](https://github.com/anl331/gooey-search-tabs)** — A morphing search bar with animated tab navigation for React. [Live Demo](https://gooey-search-tabs.vercel.app)

## License

[MIT](./LICENSE)

```
